master-ball.effect: Catches a wild Pokémon every time.
master-ball.flavor: The best Poké Ball with the ultimate level of performance. With it, you will catch any wild Pokémon without fail.

ultra-ball.effect: Tries to catch a wild Pokémon.  Success rate is 2×.
ultra-ball.flavor: An ultra-high-performance Poké Ball that provides a higher success rate for catching Pokémon than a Great Ball.

great-ball.effect: Tries to catch a wild Pokémon.  Success rate is 1.5×.
great-ball.flavor: A good, high-performance Poké Ball that provides a higher success rate for catching Pokémon than a standard Poké Ball.

poke-ball.effect: Tries to catch a wild Pokémon.
poke-ball.flavor: A device for catching wild Pokémon. It’s thrown like a ball at a Pokémon, comfortably encapsulating its target.

safari-ball.effect: Tries to catch a wild Pokémon in the Great Marsh or Safari Zone.  Success rate is 1.5×.
safari-ball.flavor: A special Poké Ball that was used in the Safari Zone in the Kanto region and in the Great Marsh in the Sinnoh region.

net-ball.effect: Tries to catch a wild Pokémon.  Success rate is 3× for water and bug Pokémon.
net-ball.flavor: A somewhat different Poké Ball that is more effective when attempting to catch Water- or Bug-type Pokémon.

dive-ball.effect: Tries to catch a wild Pokémon. Success rate is 3.5× when underwater, fishing, or surfing.
dive-ball.flavor: A somewhat different Poké Ball that works especially well when catching Pokémon that live underwater.

nest-ball.effect: Tries to catch a wild Pokémon.  Success rate is 3.9× for level 1 Pokémon, and drops steadily to 1× at level 30.
nest-ball.flavor: A somewhat different Poké Ball that becomes more effective the lower the level of the wild Pokémon.

repeat-ball.effect: Tries to catch a wild Pokémon.  Success rate is 3× for previously-caught Pokémon.
repeat-ball.flavor: A somewhat different Poké Ball that works especially well on a Pokémon species that has been caught before.

timer-ball.effect: Tries to catch a wild Pokémon. Success rate increases by 0.1× (Gen V: 0.3×) every turn, to a max of 4×.
timer-ball.flavor: A somewhat different Poké Ball that becomes progressively more effective at catching Pokémon the more turns that are taken in battle.

luxury-ball.effect: Tries to catch a wild Pokémon.  Caught Pokémon start with 200 happiness.
luxury-ball.flavor: A particularly comfortable Poké Ball that makes a wild Pokémon quickly grow friendlier after being caught.

premier-ball.effect: Tries to catch a wild Pokémon.
premier-ball.flavor: A somewhat rare Poké Ball that was made as a commemorative item used to celebrate an event of some sort.

dusk-ball.effect: Tries to catch a wild Pokémon.  Success rate is 3.5× at night and in caves.
dusk-ball.flavor: A somewhat different Poké Ball that makes it easier to catch wild Pokémon at night or in dark places such as caves.

heal-ball.effect: Tries to catch a wild Pokémon.  Caught Pokémon are immediately healed.
heal-ball.flavor: A remedial Poké Ball that restores the HP of a Pokémon caught with it and eliminates any status conditions.

quick-ball.effect: Tries to catch a wild Pokémon. Success rate is 4× (Gen V: 5×), but only on the first turn.
quick-ball.flavor: A somewhat different Poké Ball that has a more successful catch rate if used at the start of a wild encounter.

cherish-ball.effect: Tries to catch a wild Pokémon.
cherish-ball.flavor: A quite rare Poké Ball that has been crafted in order to commemorate a special occasion of some sort.

potion.flavor: A spray-type medicine for treating wounds. It can be used to restore 20 HP to a single Pokémon.

antidote.flavor: A spray-type medicine for treating poisoning. It can be used to lift the effects of being poisoned from a single Pokémon.

burn-heal.flavor: A spray-type medicine for treating burns. It can be used to heal a single Pokémon suffering from a burn.

ice-heal.flavor: A spray-type medicine for treating freezing. It can be used to thaw out a single Pokémon that has been frozen solid.

awakening.flavor: A spray-type medicine to wake the sleeping. It can be used to rouse a single Pokémon from the clutches of sleep.

paralyze-heal.flavor: A spray-type medicine for treating paralysis. It can be used to free a single Pokémon that has been paralyzed.

full-restore.effect: Restores HP to full and cures any status ailment and confusion.
full-restore.flavor: A medicine that can be used to fully restore the HP of a single Pokémon and heal any status conditions it has.

max-potion.flavor: A spray-type medicine for treating wounds. It can be used to completely restore the max HP of a single Pokémon.

hyper-potion.flavor: A spray-type medicine for treating wounds. It can be used to restore 120 HP to a single Pokémon.

super-potion.flavor: A spray-type medicine for treating wounds. It can be used to restore 60 HP to a single Pokémon.

full-heal.effect: Cures any status ailment and confusion.
full-heal.flavor: A spray-type medicine that is broadly effective. It can be used to heal all the status conditions of a single Pokémon.

revive.flavor: A medicine that can be used to revive a single Pokémon that has fainted. It also restores half of the Pokémon’s max HP.

max-revive.flavor: A medicine that can be used to revive a single Pokémon that has fainted. It also fully restores the Pokémon’s max HP.

fresh-water.flavor: Water with high mineral content. It can be used to restore 30 HP to a single Pokémon.

soda-pop.flavor: A highly carbonated soda drink. It can be used to restore 50 HP to a single Pokémon.

lemonade.flavor: A very sweet and refreshing drink. It can be used to restore 70 HP to a single Pokémon.

moomoo-milk.flavor: A bottle of highly nutritious milk. It can be used to restore 100 HP to a single Pokémon.

energy-powder.flavor: A very bitter medicinal powder. It can be used to restore 60 HP to a single Pokémon.

energy-root.flavor: An extremely bitter medicinal root. It can be used to restore 120 HP to a single Pokémon.

heal-powder.effect: Cures any status ailment, but lowers happiness.
heal-powder.flavor: A very bitter medicinal powder. It can be used once to heal all the status conditions of a Pokémon.

revival-herb.effect: Revives with full HP, but lowers happiness.
revival-herb.flavor: A terribly bitter medicinal herb. It revives a fainted Pokémon and fully restores its max HP.

ether.flavor: This medicine can be used to restore 10 PP to a single selected move that has been learned by a Pokémon.

max-ether.effect: Restores PP to full for one move.
max-ether.flavor: This medicine can be used to fully restore the PP of a single selected move that has been learned by a Pokémon.

elixir.flavor: This medicine can be used to restore 10 PP to each of the moves that have been learned by a Pokémon.

max-elixir.effect: Restores PP to full for each move.
max-elixir.flavor: This medicine can be used to fully restore the PP of all of the moves that have been learned by a Pokémon.

lava-cookie.effect: Cures any status ailment and confusion.
lava-cookie.flavor: Lavaridge Town’s local specialty. It can be used once to heal all the status conditions of a Pokémon.

berry-juice.flavor: A 100 percent pure juice made of Berries. It can be used to restore 20 HP to a single Pokémon.

sacred-ash.effect: Revives all fainted Pokémon with full HP.
sacred-ash.flavor: This rare ash can revive all fainted Pokémon in a party. In doing so, it also fully restores their max HP.

hp-up.flavor: A nutritious drink for Pokémon. When consumed, it raises the HP base points of a single Pokémon.

protein.flavor: A nutritious drink for Pokémon. When consumed, it raises the Attack base points of a single Pokémon.

iron.flavor: A nutritious drink for Pokémon. When consumed, it raises the Defense base points of a single Pokémon.

carbos.flavor: A nutritious drink for Pokémon. When consumed, it raises the Speed base points of a single Pokémon.

calcium.effect: Raises Special Attack effort and happiness.
calcium.flavor: A nutritious drink for Pokémon. When consumed, it raises the Sp. Atk base points of a single Pokémon.

rare-candy.effect: Causes a level-up and raises happiness.
rare-candy.flavor: A candy that is packed with energy. When consumed, it will instantly raise the level of a single Pokémon by one.

pp-up.effect: Raises a move’s max PP by 20%.
pp-up.flavor: A medicine that slightly raises the max PP of a single selected move that has been learned by a Pokémon.

zinc.flavor: A nutritious drink for Pokémon. When consumed, it raises the Sp. Def base points of a single Pokémon.

pp-max.effect: Raises a move’s max PP by 60%.
pp-max.flavor: A medicine that optimally raises the max PP of a single selected move that has been learned by a Pokémon.

old-gateau.effect: Cures any status ailment and confusion.
old-gateau.flavor: The Old Chateau’s hidden specialty. It can be used once to heal all the status conditions of a Pokémon.

guard-spec.effect: Prevents stat changes in battle for five turns in battle.  Raises happiness.
guard-spec.flavor: An item that prevents stat reduction among the Trainer’s party Pokémon for five turns after it is used in battle.

dire-hit.effect: Increases the chance of a critical hit in battle.  Raises happiness.
dire-hit.flavor: An item that greatly raises the critical-hit ratio of a Pokémon during a battle. It can be used only once and wears off if the Pokémon is withdrawn.

x-attack.effect: Raises Attack by one stage in battle.  Raises happiness.
x-attack.flavor: An item that sharply boosts the Attack stat of a Pokémon during a battle. It wears off once the Pokémon is withdrawn.

x-defense.effect: Raises Defense by one stage in battle.  Raises happiness.
x-defense.flavor: An item that sharply boosts the Defense stat of a Pokémon during a battle. It wears off once the Pokémon is withdrawn.

x-speed.effect: Raises Speed by one stage in battle.  Raises happiness.
x-speed.flavor: An item that sharply boosts the Speed stat of a Pokémon during a battle. It wears off once the Pokémon is withdrawn.

x-accuracy.effect: Raises accuracy by one stage in battle.  Raises happiness.
x-accuracy.flavor: An item that sharply boosts the accuracy of a Pokémon during a battle. It wears off once the Pokémon is withdrawn.

x-sp-atk.effect: Raises Special Attack by one stage in battle.  Raises happiness.
x-sp-atk.flavor: An item that sharply boosts the Sp. Atk stat of a Pokémon during a battle. It wears off once the Pokémon is withdrawn.

x-sp-def.effect: Raises Special Defense by one stage in battle.  Raises happiness.
x-sp-def.flavor: An item that sharply boosts the Sp. Def stat of a Pokémon during a battle. It wears off once the Pokémon is withdrawn.

poke-doll.flavor: A doll that attracts the attention of a Pokémon. It guarantees escape from any battle with wild Pokémon.

fluffy-tail.flavor: A toy that attracts the attention of a Pokémon. It guarantees escape from any battle with wild Pokémon.

blue-flute.flavor: A flute made from blue glass. It awakens sleeping Pokémon.

yellow-flute.flavor: A flute made from yellow glass. It snaps Pokémon out of confusion.

red-flute.flavor: A flute made from red glass. It snaps Pokémon out of infatuation.

black-flute.effect: Halves the wild Pokémon encounter rate.
black-flute.flavor: A flute made from black glass. It makes it easier to encounter strong Pokémon in the place you use it.

white-flute.effect: Doubles the wild Pokémon encounter rate.
white-flute.flavor: A flute made from white glass. It makes it easier to encounter weak Pokémon in the place you use it.

shoal-salt.effect: No effect. Gen III: Trade four and four Shoal Shells for a Shell Bell.
shoal-salt.flavor: Salt that you found inside Shoal Cave.

shoal-shell.effect: No effect. Gen III: Trade four and four Shoal Salts for a Shell Bell.
shoal-shell.flavor: A seashell that you found inside Shoal Cave.

red-shard.effect: No effect. Can be traded for items or moves.
red-shard.flavor: A small red shard. It appears to be a fragment of some sort of implement made long ago.

blue-shard.effect: No effect. Can be traded for items or moves.
blue-shard.flavor: A small blue shard. It appears to be a fragment of some sort of implement made long ago.

yellow-shard.effect: No effect. Can be traded for items or moves.
yellow-shard.flavor: A small yellow shard. It appears to be a fragment of some sort of implement made long ago.

green-shard.effect: No effect. Can be traded for items or moves.
green-shard.flavor: A small green shard. It appears to be a fragment of some sort of implement made long ago.

super-repel.effect: For 200 steps, prevents wild encounters of level lower than your party’s lead Pokémon.
super-repel.flavor: An item that prevents any low-level wild Pokémon from jumping out at you for a while. It lasts longer than Repel.

max-repel.effect: For 250 steps, prevents wild encounters of level lower than your party’s lead Pokémon.
max-repel.flavor: An item that prevents any low-level wild Pokémon from jumping out at you for a while. It lasts longer than Super Repel.

escape-rope.effect: Transports user to the outside entrance of a cave.
escape-rope.flavor: A long and durable rope. Use it to escape instantly from locations like caves or dungeons. It can be used any number of times.

repel.effect: For 100 steps, prevents wild encounters of level lower than your party’s lead Pokémon.
repel.flavor: An item that prevents any low-level wild Pokémon from jumping out at you for a while.

sun-stone.effect: Evolves a Cottonee into Whimsicott, a Gloom into Bellossom, a Petilil into Lilligant, or a Sunkern into Sunflora.
sun-stone.flavor: A peculiar stone that can make certain species of Pokémon evolve. It burns as red as the evening sun.

moon-stone.effect: Evolves a Clefairy into Clefable, a Jigglypuff into Wigglytuff, a Munna into Musharna, a Nidorina into Nidoqueen, a Nidorino into Nidoking, or a Skitty into Delcatty.
moon-stone.flavor: A peculiar stone that can make certain species of Pokémon evolve. It is as black as the night sky.

fire-stone.effect: Evolves an Eevee into Flareon, a Growlithe into Arcanine, a Pansear into Simisear, or a Vulpix into Ninetales.
fire-stone.flavor: A peculiar stone that can make certain species of Pokémon evolve. The stone has a fiery orange heart.

thunder-stone.effect: Evolves an Eelektrik into Eelektross, an Eevee into Jolteon, or a Pikachu into Raichu.
thunder-stone.flavor: A peculiar stone that can make certain species of Pokémon evolve. It has a distinct thunderbolt pattern.

water-stone.effect: Evolves an Eevee into Vaporeon, a Lombre into Ludicolo, a Panpour into Simipour, a Poliwhirl into Poliwrath, a Shellder into Cloyster, or a Staryu into Starmie.
water-stone.flavor: A peculiar stone that can make certain species of Pokémon evolve. It is the blue of a pool of clear water.

leaf-stone.effect: Evolves an Exeggcute into Exeggutor, a Gloom into Vileplume, a Nuzleaf into Shiftry, a Pansage into Simisage, or a Weepinbell into Victreebel.
leaf-stone.flavor: A peculiar stone that can make certain species of Pokémon evolve. It has an unmistakable leaf pattern.

tiny-mushroom.effect: Fire Red and Leaf Green: Trade two for prior Level-up moves. Sell for 250 Pokédollars, or to Hungry Maid for 500 Pokédollars.
tiny-mushroom.flavor: A very small and rare mushroom. It’s popular with a certain class of collectors.

big-mushroom.effect: Fire Red and Leaf Green: Trade for prior Level-up moves. Sell for 2500 Pokédollars, or to Hungry Maid for 5000 Pokédollars.
big-mushroom.flavor: A very large and rare mushroom. It’s popular with a certain class of collectors.

pearl.effect: Sell for 700 Pokédollars, or to Ore Collector for 1400 Pokédollars.
pearl.flavor: A rather small pearl that has a very nice silvery sheen to it. It can be sold at a low price to shops.

big-pearl.effect: Sell for 3750 Pokédollars, or to Ore Collector for 7500 Pokédollars.
big-pearl.flavor: A rather large pearl that has a very nice silvery sheen. It can be sold at a high price to shops.

stardust.effect: Sell for 1000 Pokédollars, or to Ore Collector for 2000 Pokédollars.
stardust.flavor: Lovely red sand that flows between the fingers with a loose, silky feel. It can be sold at a low price to shops.

star-piece.effect: Platinum: Trade for one of each color Shard. Black and White: Trade for PP Up. Sell for 4900 Pokédollars, or to Ore Collector for 9800 Pokédollars.
star-piece.flavor: A small shard of a beautiful gem that gives off a distinctly red sparkle. It can be sold at a high price to shops.

nugget.effect: Sell for 5000 Pokédollars, or to Ore Collector for 10000 Pokédollars.
nugget.flavor: A nugget of the purest gold that gives off a lustrous gleam in direct light. It can be sold at a high price to shops.

heart-scale.effect: No effect. Can be traded for prior Level-up moves.
heart-scale.flavor: A pretty, heart-shaped scale that is extremely rare. Some people are happy to receive one.

honey.flavor: A sweet honey collected by Pokémon. It has a lush aroma and can be sold at a low price to shops.

growth-mulch.effect: Growing time of berries is reduced, but the soil dries out faster.
growth-mulch.flavor: A fertilizer for growing Berries. However, it appears to be unsuitable for the soil in the Hoenn region and has no effect.

damp-mulch.effect: Growing time of berries is increased, but the soil dries out slower.
damp-mulch.flavor: A fertilizer for growing Berries. However, it appears to be unsuitable for the soil in the Hoenn region and has no effect.

stable-mulch.effect: Berries stay on the plant for longer than their usual time.
stable-mulch.flavor: A fertilizer for growing Berries. However, it appears to be unsuitable for the soil in the Hoenn region and has no effect.

gooey-mulch.effect: Berries regrow from dead plants an increased number of times.
gooey-mulch.flavor: A fertilizer for growing Berries. However, it appears to be unsuitable for the soil in the Hoenn region and has no effect.

root-fossil.effect: Can be revived into a Lileep.
root-fossil.flavor: A fossil from a prehistoric Pokémon that once lived in the sea. It looks as if it could be part of a plant’s root.

claw-fossil.effect: Can be revived into an Anorith.
claw-fossil.flavor: A fossil from a prehistoric Pokémon that once lived in the sea. It appears to be a fragment of a claw.

helix-fossil.effect: Can be revived into an Omanyte.
helix-fossil.flavor: A fossil from a prehistoric Pokémon that once lived in the sea. It might be a piece of a seashell.

dome-fossil.effect: Can be revived into a Kabuto.
dome-fossil.flavor: A fossil from a prehistoric Pokémon that once lived in the sea. It could be a shell or carapace.

old-amber.effect: Can be revived into an Aerodactyl.
old-amber.flavor: A piece of amber that still contains the genetic material of an ancient Pokémon. It’s clear with a tawny, reddish tint.

armor-fossil.effect: Can be revived into a Shieldon.
armor-fossil.flavor: A fossil from a prehistoric Pokémon that once lived on the land. It looks to be from some kind of protective collar.

skull-fossil.effect: Can be revived into a Cranidos.
skull-fossil.flavor: A fossil from a prehistoric Pokémon that once lived on the land. It appears as though it’s part of a head.

rare-bone.effect: Sell for 5000 Pokédollars, or to Bone Man for 10000 Pokédollars.
rare-bone.flavor: A rare bone that is extremely valuable for the study of Pokémon archeology. It can be sold for a high price to shops.

shiny-stone.effect: Evolves a Minccino into Cinccino, a Roselia into Roserade, or a Togetic into Togekiss.
shiny-stone.flavor: A peculiar stone that can make certain species of Pokémon evolve. It shines with a dazzling light.

dusk-stone.effect: Evolves a Lampent into Chandelure, a Misdreavus into Mismagius, or a Murkrow into Honchkrow.
dusk-stone.flavor: A peculiar stone that can make certain species of Pokémon evolve. It holds shadows as dark as can be.

dawn-stone.effect: Evolves a male Kirlia into Gallade or a female Snorunt into Froslass.
dawn-stone.flavor: A peculiar stone that can make certain species of Pokémon evolve. It sparkles like a glittering eye.

oval-stone.effect: Level-up during Day on a Happiny: Holder evolves into Chansey.
oval-stone.flavor: A peculiar stone that can make certain species of Pokémon evolve. It’s as round as a Pokémon Egg.

odd-keystone.effect: Use on the tower on Route 209 to encounter Spiritomb if you have at least 32 Underground greetings.
odd-keystone.flavor: A vital item that is needed to keep a stone tower from collapsing. Voices can be heard from it occasionally.

adamant-orb.effect: Boosts the damage from Dialga’s Dragon-type and Steel-type moves by 20%.
adamant-orb.flavor: A brightly gleaming orb to be held by Dialga. It boosts the power of Dragon- and Steel-type moves when it is held.

lustrous-orb.effect: Boosts the damage from Palkia’s Dragon-type and Water-type moves by 20%.
lustrous-orb.flavor: A beautifully glowing orb to be held by Palkia. It boosts the power of Dragon- and Water-type moves when it is held.

grass-mail.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
grass-mail.flavor: Stationery featuring a print of a refreshingly green field. Let a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

flame-mail.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
flame-mail.flavor: Stationery featuring a print of flames in blazing red. Let a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

bubble-mail.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
bubble-mail.flavor: Stationery featuring a print of a blue world underwater. Let a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

bloom-mail.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
bloom-mail.flavor: Stationery featuring a print of pretty floral patterns. Let a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

tunnel-mail.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
tunnel-mail.flavor: Stationery featuring a print of a dimly lit coal mine. Let a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

steel-mail.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
steel-mail.flavor: Stationery featuring a print of cool mechanical designs. Let a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

heart-mail.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
heart-mail.flavor: Stationery featuring a print of giant heart patterns. Let a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

snow-mail.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
snow-mail.flavor: Stationery featuring a print of a chilly, snow-covered world. Let a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

space-mail.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
space-mail.flavor: Stationery featuring a print depicting the huge expanse of space. Let a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

air-mail.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
air-mail.flavor: Stationery featuring a print of colorful letter sets. Let a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

mosaic-mail.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
mosaic-mail.flavor: Stationery featuring a print of a vivid rainbow pattern. Let a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

brick-mail.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
brick-mail.flavor: Stationery featuring a print of a tough-looking brick pattern. Let a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

cheri-berry.effect: Held: Consumed when paralyzed to cure paralysis.
cheri-berry.flavor: A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon holds one, it can recover from paralysis on its own in battle.

chesto-berry.effect: Held: Consumed when asleep to cure sleep.
chesto-berry.flavor: A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon holds one, it can recover from sleep on its own in battle.

pecha-berry.effect: Held: Consumed when poisoned to cure poison.
pecha-berry.flavor: A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon holds one, it can recover from poisoning on its own in battle.

rawst-berry.effect: Held: Consumed when burned to cure a burn.
rawst-berry.flavor: A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon holds one, it can recover from a burn on its own in battle.

aspear-berry.effect: Held: Consumed when frozen to cure frozen.
aspear-berry.flavor: A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon holds one, it can recover from being frozen on its own in battle.

leppa-berry.effect: Held: Consumed when a move runs out of PP to restore its PP by 10.
leppa-berry.flavor: A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon holds one, it can restore 10 PP to a depleted move during battle.

oran-berry.effect: Held: Consumed at 1/2 max HP to recover 10 HP.
oran-berry.flavor: A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon holds one, it can restore its own HP by 10 points during battle.

persim-berry.effect: Held: Consumed when confused to cure confusion.
persim-berry.flavor: A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon holds one, it can recover from confusion on its own in battle.

lum-berry.effect: Held: Consumed to cure any status condition or confusion.
lum-berry.flavor: A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon holds one, it can recover from any status condition during battle.

sitrus-berry.effect: Held: Consumed at 1/2 max HP to recover 1/4 max HP.
sitrus-berry.flavor: A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon holds one, it can restore its own HP by a small amount during battle.

figy-berry.effect: Held: Consumed at 1/2 max HP to restore 1/8 max HP. Confuses Pokémon that dislike spicy flavor.
figy-berry.flavor: If held by a Pokémon, it restores the user’s HP in a pinch, but it will cause confusion if the user hates the taste.

wiki-berry.effect: Held: Consumed at 1/2 max HP to restore 1/8 max HP. Confuses Pokémon that dislike dry flavor.
wiki-berry.flavor: If held by a Pokémon, it restores the user’s HP in a pinch, but it will cause confusion if the user hates the taste.

mago-berry.effect: Held: Consumed at 1/2 max HP to restore 1/8 max HP. Confuses Pokémon that dislike sweet flavor.
mago-berry.flavor: If held by a Pokémon, it restores the user’s HP in a pinch, but it will cause confusion if the user hates the taste.

aguav-berry.effect: Held: Consumed at 1/2 max HP to restore 1/8 max HP. Confuses Pokémon that dislike bitter flavor.
aguav-berry.flavor: If held by a Pokémon, it restores the user’s HP in a pinch, but it will cause confusion if the user hates the taste.

iapapa-berry.effect: Held: Consumed at 1/2 max HP to restore 1/8 max HP. Confuses Pokémon that dislike sour flavor.
iapapa-berry.flavor: If held by a Pokémon, it restores the user’s HP in a pinch, but it will cause confusion if the user hates the taste.

razz-berry.effect: Used for creating PokéBlocks and Poffins.
razz-berry.flavor: Used to make Pokéblocks that will enhance your Coolness. Its red flesh is spicy when eaten.

bluk-berry.effect: Used for creating PokéBlocks and Poffins.
bluk-berry.flavor: Used to make Pokéblocks that will enhance your Beauty. Its blue flesh is dry when eaten.

nanab-berry.effect: Used for creating PokéBlocks and Poffins.
nanab-berry.flavor: Used to make Pokéblocks that will enhance your Cuteness. Its pink flesh is sweet when eaten.

wepear-berry.effect: Used for creating PokéBlocks and Poffins.
wepear-berry.flavor: Used to make Pokéblocks that will enhance your Cleverness. Its green flesh is bitter when eaten.

pinap-berry.effect: Used for creating PokéBlocks and Poffins.
pinap-berry.flavor: Used to make Pokéblocks that will enhance your Toughness. Its yellow flesh is sour when eaten.

pomeg-berry.effect: Drops HP Effort Values by 10 and raises happiness.
pomeg-berry.flavor: A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. Using it on a Pokémon makes it more friendly but lowers its HP base points.

kelpsy-berry.effect: Drops Attack Effort Values by 10 and raises happiness.
kelpsy-berry.flavor: A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. Using it on a Pokémon makes it more friendly but lowers its Attack base points.

qualot-berry.effect: Drops Defense Effort Values by 10 and raises happiness.
qualot-berry.flavor: A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. Using it on a Pokémon makes it more friendly but lowers its Defense base points.

hondew-berry.effect: Drops Special Attack Effort Values by 10 and raises happiness.
hondew-berry.flavor: A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. Using it on a Pokémon makes it more friendly but lowers its Sp. Atk base points.

grepa-berry.effect: Drops Special Defense Effort Values by 10 and raises happiness.
grepa-berry.flavor: A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. Using it on a Pokémon makes it more friendly but lowers its Sp. Def base points.

tamato-berry.effect: Drops Speed Effort Values by 10 and raises happiness.
tamato-berry.flavor: A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. Using it on a Pokémon makes it more friendly but lowers its Speed base points.

cornn-berry.effect: Used for creating PokéBlocks and Poffins.

chilan-berry.flavor: If held by a Pokémon, this Berry will lessen the damage taken from one Normal-type attack.
