liechi-berry.flavor: A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon holds one, its Attack stat will increase when it’s in a pinch.

ganlon-berry.effect: Held: Consumed at 1/4 max HP to boost Defense.
ganlon-berry.flavor: A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon holds one, its Defense stat will increase when it’s in a pinch.

salac-berry.effect: Held: Consumed at 1/4 max HP to boost Speed.
salac-berry.flavor: A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon holds one, its Speed stat will increase when it’s in a pinch.

petaya-berry.effect: Held: Consumed at 1/4 max HP to boost Special Attack.
petaya-berry.flavor: A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon holds one, its Sp. Atk stat will increase when it’s in a pinch.

apicot-berry.effect: Held: Consumed at 1/4 max HP to boost Special Defense.
apicot-berry.flavor: A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon holds one, its Sp. Def stat will increase when it’s in a pinch.

lansat-berry.effect: Held: Consumed at 1/4 max HP to boost critical hit ratio by two stages.
lansat-berry.flavor: A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon holds one, its critical-hit ratio will increase when it’s in a pinch.

starf-berry.effect: Held: Consumed at 1/4 max HP to boost a random stat by two stages.
starf-berry.flavor: A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon holds one, one of its stats will sharply increase when it’s in a pinch.

enigma-berry.effect: Held: Consumed when struck by a super-effective attack to restore 1/4 max HP.
enigma-berry.flavor: A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon holds one, being hit by a supereffective attack will restore its HP.

micle-berry.effect: Held: Consumed at 1/4 max HP to boost accuracy of next move by 20%. (Gen IV: Perfect accuracy)
micle-berry.flavor: A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon holds one, its accuracy will increase just once when it’s in a pinch.

custap-berry.effect: Held: Consumed at 1/4 max HP when using a move to go first.
custap-berry.flavor: A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon holds one, it will be able to move first just once when it’s in a pinch.

jaboca-berry.effect: Held: Consumed to deal 1/8 attacker’s max HP when holder is struck by a physical attack.
jaboca-berry.flavor: A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a physical attack hits the Pokémon holding it, the attacker will also be hurt.

rowap-berry.effect: Held: Consumed to deal 1/8 attacker’s max HP when holder is struck by a special attack.
rowap-berry.flavor: A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a special attack hits the Pokémon holding it, the attacker will also be hurt.

bright-powder.effect: Held: Increases the holder’s evasion by 1/9 (11 1/9%).
bright-powder.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It casts a tricky glare that lowers the opposing Pokémon’s accuracy.

white-herb.effect: Held: Resets all lowered stats to normal at end of turn. Consumed after use.
white-herb.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It will restore any lowered stat in battle. It can be used only once.

macho-brace.effect: Held: Holder gains double effort values from battles, but has halved Speed in battle.
macho-brace.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. This stiff, heavy brace helps Pokémon grow strong but cuts Speed in battle.

exp-share.effect: Held: Half the experience from a battle is split between Pokémon holding this item.
exp-share.flavor: Turning on this special device will allow all the Pokémon on your team to receive Exp. Points from battles.

quick-claw.effect: Held: Holder has a 3/16 (18.75%) chance to move first.
quick-claw.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. This light, sharp claw lets the bearer move first occasionally.

soothe-bell.effect: Held: Doubles the happiness earned by the holder.
soothe-bell.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. The comforting chime of this bell calms the holder, making it friendly.

mental-herb.effect: Held: Consumed to cure infatuation. Gen V: Also removes Taunt, Encore, Torment, Disable, and Cursed Body.
mental-herb.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. The holder shakes off move-binding effects to move freely. It can be used only once.

choice-band.effect: Held: Increases Attack by 50%, but restricts the holder to only one move.
choice-band.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. This curious headband boosts Attack but only allows the use of one move.

kings-rock.effect: Held: Damaging moves gain a 10% chance to make their target flinch. Traded on a Poliwhirl: Holder evolves into Politoed. Traded on a Slowpoke: Holder evolves into Slowking.
kings-rock.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. When the holder successfully inflicts damage, the target may also flinch.

silver-powder.effect: Held: Bug-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
silver-powder.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a shiny silver powder that will boost the power of Bug-type moves.

amulet-coin.effect: Held: Doubles the money earned from a battle. Does not stack with Luck Incense.
amulet-coin.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It doubles any prize money received if the holding Pokémon joins a battle.

cleanse-tag.effect: Prevents wild encounters of level lower than your party’s lead Pokémon.
cleanse-tag.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It helps keep wild Pokémon away if the holder is the head of the party.

soul-dew.effect: Raises Latias and Latios’s Special Attack and Special Defense by 50%.
soul-dew.flavor: A wondrous orb to be held by either Latios or Latias. It raises the power of Psychic- and Dragon-type moves.

deep-sea-tooth.effect: Doubles Clamperl’s Special Attack. Traded on a Clamperl: Holder evolves into Huntail.
deep-sea-tooth.flavor: An item to be held by Clamperl. This fang gleams a sharp silver and raises the holder’s Sp. Atk stat.

deep-sea-scale.effect: Doubles Clamperl’s Special Defense. Traded on a Clamperl: Holder evolves into Gorebyss.
deep-sea-scale.flavor: An item to be held by Clamperl. This scale shines with a faint pink and raises the holder’s Sp. Def stat.

smoke-ball.effect: Held: Allows the Holder to escape from any wild battle.
smoke-ball.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It enables the holder to flee from any wild Pokémon encounter without fail.

everstone.effect: Held: Prevents level-based evolution from occuring.
everstone.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. A Pokémon holding this peculiar stone is prevented from evolving.

focus-band.effect: Held: Holder has 10% chance to survive attacks or self-inflicted damage at 1 HP.
focus-band.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. The holder may endure a potential KO attack, leaving it with just 1 HP.

lucky-egg.effect: Held: Increases EXP earned in battle by 50%.
lucky-egg.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s an egg filled with happiness that earns the holder extra Exp. Points in battle.

scope-lens.effect: Held: Raises the holder’s critical hit ratio by one stage.
scope-lens.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a lens for scoping out weak points. It boosts the holder’s critical-hit ratio.

metal-coat.effect: Held: Steel-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
metal-coat.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a special metallic film that can boost the power of Steel-type moves.

leftovers.effect: Held: Restores 1/16 (6.25%) holder’s max HP at the end of each turn.
leftovers.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. The holder’s HP is slowly but steadily restored throughout every battle.

dragon-scale.effect: Traded on a Seadra: Holder evolves into Kingdra.
dragon-scale.flavor: A very tough and inflexible scale. Dragon-type Pokémon may be holding this item when caught.

light-ball.effect: Doubles Pikachu’s Attack and Special Attack. Breed on Pikachu or Raichu: Pichu Egg will have Volt Tackle.
light-ball.flavor: An item to be held by Pikachu. It’s a puzzling orb that boosts its Attack and Sp. Atk stats.

soft-sand.effect: Held: Ground-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
soft-sand.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a loose, silky sand that boosts the power of Ground-type moves.

hard-stone.effect: Held: Rock-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
hard-stone.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a durable stone that boosts the power of Rock-type moves.

miracle-seed.effect: Held: Grass-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
miracle-seed.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a seed imbued with life-force that boosts the power of Grass-type moves.

black-glasses.effect: Held: Dark-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
black-glasses.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a pair of shady-looking glasses that boost the power of Dark-type moves.

black-belt.effect: Held: Fighting-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
black-belt.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. This belt helps the wearer to focus and boosts the power of Fighting-type moves.

magnet.effect: Held: Electric-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
magnet.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a powerful magnet that boosts the power of Electric-type moves.

mystic-water.effect: Held: Water-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
mystic-water.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. This teardrop-shaped gem boosts the power of Water-type moves.

sharp-beak.effect: Held: Flying-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
sharp-beak.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a long, sharp beak that boosts the power of Flying-type moves.

poison-barb.effect: Held: Poison-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
poison-barb.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. This small, poisonous barb boosts the power of Poison-type moves.

never-melt-ice.effect: Held: Ice-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
never-melt-ice.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a heat- repelling piece of ice that boosts the power of Ice-type moves.

spell-tag.effect: Held: Ghost-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
spell-tag.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a sinister, eerie tag that boosts the power of Ghost-type moves.

twisted-spoon.effect: Held: Psychic-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
twisted-spoon.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. This spoon is imbued with telekinetic power and boosts Psychic-type moves.

charcoal.effect: Held: Fire-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
charcoal.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a combustible fuel that boosts the power of Fire-type moves.

dragon-fang.effect: Held: Dragon-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
dragon-fang.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. This hard and sharp fang boosts the power of Dragon-type moves.

silk-scarf.effect: Held: Normal-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
silk-scarf.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a sumptuous scarf that boosts the power of Normal-type moves.

up-grade.effect: Traded on a Porygon: Holder evolves into Porygon2.
up-grade.flavor: A transparent device somehow filled with all sorts of data. It was produced by Silph Co.

shell-bell.effect: Held: Holder receives 1/8 of the damage it deals when attacking.
shell-bell.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. The holder restores a little HP every time it inflicts damage on others.

sea-incense.effect: Held: Water-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Breeding: Marill or Azumarill beget an Azurill Egg.
sea-incense.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. This incense has a curious aroma that boosts the power of Water-type moves.

lax-incense.effect: Held: Holder’s evasion is increased by 5%. Breeding: Wobbuffet begets a Wynaut Egg.
lax-incense.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. The beguiling aroma of this incense may cause attacks to miss its holder.

lucky-punch.effect: Raises Chansey’s critical hit ratio by two stages.
lucky-punch.flavor: An item to be held by Chansey. This pair of lucky boxing gloves will boost Chansey’s critical-hit ratio.

metal-powder.effect: Raises Ditto’s Defense and Special Defense by 50%. The boost is lost after transforming.
metal-powder.flavor: An item to be held by Ditto. Extremely fine yet hard, this odd powder boosts the Defense stat.

thick-club.flavor: An item to be held by Cubone or Marowak. It’s a hard bone of some sort that boosts the Attack stat.

stick.effect: Raises Farfetch’d’s critical hit ratio by two stages.
stick.flavor: An item to be held by Farfetch’d. This very long and stiff stalk of leek boosts its critical-hit ratio.

red-scarf.effect: Raises the holder’s Coolness while in a contest.
red-scarf.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. During a contest, it raises the Cool aspect of the Pokémon holding it.

blue-scarf.effect: Raises the holder’s Beauty while in a contest.
blue-scarf.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. During a contest, it raises the Beauty aspect of the Pokémon holding it.

pink-scarf.effect: Raises the holder’s Cuteness while in a contest.
pink-scarf.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. During a contest, it raises the Cute aspect of the Pokémon holding it.

green-scarf.effect: Raises the holder’s Smartness while in a contest.
green-scarf.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. During a contest, it raises the Clever aspect of the Pokémon holding it.

yellow-scarf.effect: Raises the holder’s Toughness while in a contest.
yellow-scarf.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. During a contest, it raises the Tough aspect of the Pokémon holding it.

wide-lens.effect: Held: Provides a 1/10 (10%) boost in accuracy to the holder.
wide-lens.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a magnifying lens that slightly boosts the accuracy of moves.

muscle-band.effect: Held: Boosts the damage of physical moves used by the holder by 10%.
muscle-band.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. This headband exudes strength, slightly boosting the power of physical moves.

wise-glasses.effect: Held: Boosts the damage of special moves used by the holder by 1/10 (10%).
wise-glasses.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. This thick pair of glasses slightly boosts the power of special moves.

expert-belt.effect: Held: Holder’s Super Effective moves do 20% extra damage.
expert-belt.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a well-worn belt that slightly boosts the power of supereffective moves.

light-clay.effect: Held: Light Screen and Reflect used by the holder last 8 rounds instead of 5.
light-clay.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. Protective moves like Light Screen and Reflect will be effective longer.

life-orb.effect: Held: Holder’s moves inflict 30% extra damage, but cost 10% max HP.
life-orb.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It boosts the power of moves but at the cost of some HP on each hit.

power-herb.effect: Held: Both turns of a two-turn charge move happen at once. Consumed upon use.
power-herb.flavor: A single-use item to be held by a Pokémon. It allows the holder to immediately use a move that normally requires a turn to charge.

toxic-orb.effect: Held: Inflicts Toxic on the holder at the end of the turn. Activates after Poison damage would occur.
toxic-orb.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a bizarre orb that will badly poison the holder during battle.

flame-orb.effect: Held: Inflicts Burn on the holder at the end of the turn. Activates after Burn damage would occur.
flame-orb.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a bizarre orb that will afflict the holder with a burn during battle.

quick-powder.effect: Doubles Ditto’s Speed when held. The boost is lost after transforming.
quick-powder.flavor: An item to be held by Ditto. Extremely fine yet hard, this odd powder boosts the Speed stat.

focus-sash.effect: Held: Holder survives any single-hit attack at 1 HP if at max HP, then the item is consumed.
focus-sash.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. If the holder has full HP, it will endure a potential KO attack with 1 HP. The item then disappears.

zoom-lens.effect: Held: Provides a 1/5 (20%) boost in accuracy if the holder moves after the target.
zoom-lens.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. If the holder moves after its target moves, its accuracy will be boosted.

metronome.effect: Held: Consectutive uses of the same attack have a cumulative damage boost of 10%. Maximum 100% boost.
metronome.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It boosts moves used consecutively but only until a different move is used.

iron-ball.effect: Held: Holder’s Speed is halved. Negates all Ground-type immunities, and makes Flying-types take neutral damage from Ground-type moves. Arena Trap. Spikes, and Toxic Spikes affect the holder.
iron-ball.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It lowers Speed and allows Ground-type moves to hit Flying types and holders that are levitating.

lagging-tail.effect: Held: Holder moves last in its priority bracket.
lagging-tail.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is tremendously heavy and makes the holder move slower than usual.

destiny-knot.effect: Held: Infatuates opposing Pokémon when holder is inflicted with infatuation.
destiny-knot.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. If the holder becomes infatuated, the opposing Pokémon will be, too.

black-sludge.effect: Held: Poison-type holder recovers 1/16 (6.25%) max HP each turn. Non-Poison-Types take 1/8 (12.5%) max HP damage.
black-sludge.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It gradually restores HP to Poison-type Pokémon. It damages any other type.

icy-rock.effect: Held: Hail by the holder lasts 8 rounds instead of 5.
icy-rock.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It extends the duration of the move Hail when used by the holder.

smooth-rock.effect: Held: Sandstorm by the holder lasts 8 rounds instead of 5.
smooth-rock.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It extends the duration of the move Sandstorm when used by the holder.

heat-rock.effect: Held: Sunny Day by the holder lasts 8 rounds instead of 5.
heat-rock.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It extends the duration of the move Sunny Day when used by the holder.

damp-rock.effect: Held: Rain Dance by the holder lasts 8 rounds instead of 5.
damp-rock.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It extends the duration of the move Rain Dance when used by the holder.

grip-claw.effect: Held: Holder’s multi-turn trapping moves last 5 turns.
grip-claw.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It extends the duration of multi-turn attacks, such as Bind and Wrap.

choice-scarf.effect: Held: Increases Speed by 50%, but restricts the holder to only one move.
choice-scarf.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. This curious scarf boosts Speed but only allows the use of one move.

sticky-barb.effect: Held: Holder takes 1/8 (12.5%) its max HP at the end of each turn. When the holder is hit by a contact move, the attacking Pokémon takes 1/8 its max HP in damage and receive the item if not holding one.
sticky-barb.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It damages the holder every turn and may latch on to Pokémon that touch the holder.

power-bracer.effect: Held: Holder gains 4 Attack effort values, but has halved Speed in battle.
power-bracer.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It reduces Speed but allows the holder’s Attack stat to grow more after battling.

power-belt.effect: Held: Holder gains 4 Defense effort values, but has halved Speed in battle.
power-belt.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It reduces Speed but allows the holder’s Defense stat to grow more after battling.

power-lens.effect: Held: Holder gains 4 Special Attack effort values, but has halved Speed in battle.
power-lens.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It reduces Speed but allows the holder’s Sp. Atk stat to grow more after battling.

power-band.effect: Held: Holder gains 4 Special Defense effort values, but has halved Speed in battle.
power-band.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It reduces Speed but allows the holder’s Sp. Def stat to grow more after battling.

power-anklet.effect: Held: Holder gains 4 Speed effort values, but has halved Speed in battle.
power-anklet.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It reduces Speed but allows the holder’s Speed stat to grow more after battling.

power-weight.effect: Held: Holder gains 4 HP effort values, but has halved Speed in battle.
power-weight.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It reduces Speed but allows the holder’s max HP to grow more after battling.

shed-shell.effect: Held: Holder can bypass all trapping effects and switch out. Multi-turn moves still cannot be switched out of.
shed-shell.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. This discarded carapace enables the holder to switch out of battle without fail.

big-root.effect: Held: Increases HP recovered from draining moves, Ingrain, and Aqua Ring by 3/10 (30%).
big-root.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It boosts the amount of HP the holder restores from HP-stealing moves.

choice-specs.effect: Held: Increases Special Attack by 50%, but restricts the holder to only one move.
choice-specs.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. These curious glasses boost Sp. Atk but only allow the use of one move.

flame-plate.effect: Held: Fire-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Fire.
flame-plate.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet that boosts the power of Fire-type moves.

splash-plate.effect: Held: Water-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Water.
splash-plate.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet that boosts the power of Water-type moves.

zap-plate.effect: Held: Electric-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Electric.
zap-plate.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet that boosts the power of Electric-type moves.

meadow-plate.effect: Held: Grass-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Grass.
meadow-plate.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet that boosts the power of Grass-type moves.

icicle-plate.effect: Held: Ice-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Ice.
icicle-plate.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet that boosts the power of Ice-type moves.

fist-plate.effect: Held: Fighting-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Fighting.
fist-plate.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet that boosts the power of Fighting-type moves.

toxic-plate.effect: Held: Poison-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Poison.
toxic-plate.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet that boosts the power of Poison-type moves.

earth-plate.effect: Held: Ground-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Ground.
earth-plate.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet that boosts the power of Ground-type moves.

sky-plate.effect: Held: Flying-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Flying.
sky-plate.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet that boosts the power of Flying-type moves.

mind-plate.effect: Held: Psychic-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Psychic.
mind-plate.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet that boosts the power of Psychic-type moves.

insect-plate.effect: Held: Bug-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Bug.
insect-plate.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet that boosts the power of Bug-type moves.

stone-plate.effect: Held: Rock-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Rock.
stone-plate.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet that boosts the power of Rock-type moves.

spooky-plate.effect: Held: Ghost-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Ghost.
spooky-plate.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet that boosts the power of Ghost-type moves.

draco-plate.effect: Held: Dragon-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Dragon.
draco-plate.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet that boosts the power of Dragon-type moves.

dread-plate.effect: Held: Dark-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Dark.
dread-plate.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet that boosts the power of Dark-type moves.

iron-plate.effect: Held: Steel-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Steel.
iron-plate.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet that boosts the power of Steel-type moves.

odd-incense.effect: Held: Psychic-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Breeding: Mr. Mime begets a Mime Jr. Egg.
odd-incense.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. This exotic-smelling incense boosts the power of Psychic-type moves.

rock-incense.effect: Held: Rock-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Breeding: Sudowoodo begets a Bonsly Egg.
rock-incense.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. This exotic-smelling incense boosts the power of Rock-type moves.

full-incense.effect: Held: Holder moves last in its priority bracket. Breeding: Snorlax begets a Munchlax Egg.
full-incense.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. This exotic-smelling incense makes the holder bloated and slow moving.

wave-incense.effect: Held: Water-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Breeding: Mantine begets a Mantyke Egg.
wave-incense.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. This incense has a curious aroma that boosts the power of Water-type moves.

rose-incense.effect: Held: Grass-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Breeding: Roselia or Roserade beget a Budew Egg.
rose-incense.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. This exotic-smelling incense boosts the power of Grass-type moves.

luck-incense.effect: Held: Doubles the money earned from a battle. Does not stack with Amulet Coin. Breeding: Chansey and Blissey beget a Happiny Egg.
luck-incense.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It doubles any prize money received if the holding Pokémon joins a battle.

pure-incense.effect: Prevents wild encounters of level lower than your party’s lead Pokémon. Breeding: Chimecho begets a Chingling Egg.
pure-incense.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It helps keep wild Pokémon away if the holder is the head of the party.

protector.effect: Traded on a Rhydon: Holder evolves into Rhyperior.
protector.flavor: A protective item of some sort. It is extremely stiff and heavy. It’s loved by a certain Pokémon.

electirizer.effect: Traded on an Electabuzz: Holder evolves into Electivire.
electirizer.flavor: A box packed with a tremendous amount of electric energy. It’s loved by a certain Pokémon.

magmarizer.effect: Traded on a Magmar: Holder evolves into Magmortar.
magmarizer.flavor: A box packed with a tremendous amount of magma energy. It’s loved by a certain Pokémon.

dubious-disc.effect: Traded on a Porygon2: Holder evolves into Porygon-Z.
dubious-disc.flavor: A transparent device overflowing with dubious data. Its producer is unknown.

reaper-cloth.effect: Traded on a Dusclops: Holder evolves into Dusknoir.
reaper-cloth.flavor: A cloth imbued with horrifyingly strong spiritual energy. It’s loved by a certain Pokémon.

razor-claw.effect: Held: Raises the holder’s critical hit ratio by one stage. Held by a Sneasel while levelling up at night: Holder evolves into Weavile.
razor-claw.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. This sharply hooked claw increases the holder’s critical-hit ratio.

razor-fang.effect: Held: Damaging moves gain a 10% chance to make their target flinch. Held by a Gligar while levelling up: Holder evolves into Gliscor.
razor-fang.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. When the holder successfully inflicts damage, the target may also flinch.

tm01.effect: Teaches Hone Claws to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV & III: Focus Punch Gen II: DynamicPunch Gen I: Mega Punch)
tm01.flavor: The target is attacked by a kick launched with muscle-packed power.

tm02.effect: Teaches Dragon Claw to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II: Headbutt Gen I: Razor Wind)
tm02.flavor: Numerous coins are hurled at the target to inflict damage. Money is earned after the battle.

tm03.effect: Teaches Psyshock to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV & III: Water Pulse Gen II: Curse Gen I: Swords Dance)
tm03.flavor: The target is punched with a fiery fist. This may also leave the target with a burn.

tm04.effect: Teaches Calm Mind to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II: Rollout Gen I: Whirlwind)
tm04.flavor: The target is punched with an icy fist. This may also leave the target frozen.

tm05.effect: Teaches Roar to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen I: Mega Kick)
tm05.flavor: The target is punched with an electrified fist. This may also leave the target with paralysis.

tm06.effect: Teaches Toxic to a compatible Pokémon.
tm06.flavor: The user flies up into the sky and then strikes its target on the next turn.

tm07.effect: Teaches Hail to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II: Zap Cannon Gen I: Horn Drill)
tm07.flavor: Sharp spikes are shot at the target in rapid succession. They hit two to five times in a row.

tm08.effect: Teaches Bulk Up to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II: Rock Smash Gen I: Body Slam)
tm08.flavor: The target is attacked with a powerful beam. The user can’t move on the next turn.

tm09.effect: Teaches Venoshock to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV & III: Bullet Seed Gen II: Psych Up Gen I: Take Down)
tm09.flavor: The user charges at the target using every bit of its power. The user can’t move on the next turn.

tm10.effect: Teaches Hidden Power to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen I: Double-Edge)
tm10.flavor: The user scatters curious leaves that chase the target. This attack never misses.

tm11.effect: Teaches Sunny Day to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen I: BubbleBeam)
tm11.flavor: In this two-turn attack, the user gathers light, then blasts a bundled beam on the next turn.

tm12.effect: Teaches Taunt to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II: Sweet Scent Gen I: Water Gun)
tm12.flavor: In this two-turn attack, the user gathers light and fills a blade with the light’s energy, attacking the target on the next turn.

tm13.effect: Teaches Ice Beam to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II: Snore)
tm13.flavor: The target becomes trapped within a fierce vortex of fire that rages for four to five turns.

tm14.effect: Teaches Blizzard to a compatible Pokémon.
tm14.flavor: The user launches a weak jolt of electricity that paralyzes the target.

tm15.effect: Teaches Hyper Beam to a compatible Pokémon.
tm15.flavor: The user burrows into the ground, then attacks on the next turn.

tm16.effect: Teaches Light Screen to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II: Icy Wind Gen I: Pay Day)
tm16.flavor: An earsplitting screech harshly lowers the target’s Defense stat.

tm17.effect: Teaches Protect to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen I: Submission)
tm17.flavor: A wondrous wall of light is put up to reduce damage from special attacks for five turns.

tm18.effect: Teaches Rain Dance to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen I: Counter)
tm18.flavor: A wondrous wall of light is put up to reduce damage from physical attacks for five turns.

tm19.effect: Teaches Telekinesis to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV & III & II: Giga Drain Gen I: Seismic Toss)
tm19.flavor: The user creates a protective field that prevents status conditions for five turns.

tm20.effect: Teaches Safeguard to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II: Endure Gen I: Rage)
tm20.flavor: The user attacks everything around it by causing an explosion. The user faints upon using this move.

tm21.effect: Teaches Frustration to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen I: Mega Drain)
tm21.flavor: The user goes to sleep for two turns. This fully restores the user’s HP and heals any status conditions.

tm22.effect: Teaches SolarBeam to a compatible Pokémon.
tm22.flavor: Large boulders are hurled at opposing Pokémon to inflict damage. This may also make the opposing Pokémon flinch.

tm23.effect: Teaches Smack Down to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV & III & II: Iron Tail Gen I: Dragon Rage)
tm23.flavor: The user attacks and steals the target’s held item simultaneously. The user can’t steal anything if it already holds an item.

tm24.effect: Teaches Thunderbolt to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II: DragonBreath)
tm24.flavor: This attack can be used only if the user is asleep. The harsh noise may also make the target flinch.

tm25.effect: Teaches Thunder to a compatible Pokémon.
tm25.flavor: This move enables the user to protect itself from all attacks. Its chance of failing rises if it is used in succession.

tm26.effect: Teaches Earthquake to a compatible Pokémon.
tm26.flavor: The user frightens the target with a scary face to harshly lower its Speed stat.

tm27.effect: Teaches Return to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen I: Fissure)
tm27.flavor: The user attacks with a gust of chilled air. This also lowers opposing Pokémon’s Speed stats.

tm28.effect: Teaches Dig to a compatible Pokémon.
tm28.flavor: A nutrient-draining attack. The user’s HP is restored by half the damage taken by the target.

tm29.effect: Teaches Psychic to a compatible Pokémon.
tm29.flavor: The user gazes at the target rather charmingly, making it less wary. This harshly lowers the target’s Attack stat.

tm30.effect: Teaches Shadow Ball to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen I: Teleport)
tm30.flavor: The target is hit with wings of steel. This may also raise the user’s Defense stat.

tm31.effect: Teaches Brick Break to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II: Mud-Slap Gen I: Mimic)
tm31.flavor: If it is the opposite gender of the user, the target becomes infatuated and less likely to attack.

tm32.effect: Teaches Double Team to a compatible Pokémon.
tm32.flavor: A five-turn sandstorm is summoned to hurt all combatants except Rock, Ground, and Steel types. It raises the Sp. Def stat of Rock types.

tm33.effect: Teaches Reflect to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II: Ice Punch)
tm33.flavor: The user summons a heavy rain that falls for five turns, powering up Water-type moves. It lowers the power of Fire-type moves.

tm34.effect: Teaches Sludge Wave to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV & III: Shock Wave Gen II: Swagger Gen I: Bide)
tm34.flavor: The user intensifies the sun for five turns, powering up Fire-type moves. It lowers the power of Water-type moves.

tm35.effect: Teaches Flamethrower to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II: Sleep Talk Gen I: Metronome)
tm35.flavor: The user summons a hailstorm lasting five turns. It damages all Pokémon except Ice types.

tm36.effect: Teaches Sludge Bomb to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen I: Selfdestruct)
tm36.flavor: The user traps the target in a violent swirling whirlpool for four to five turns.

tm37.effect: Teaches Sandstorm to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen I: Egg Bomb)
tm37.flavor: The user gets all party Pokémon to attack the target. The more party Pokémon, the greater the number of attacks.

tm38.effect: Teaches Fire Blast to a compatible Pokémon.
tm38.flavor: The user shoots a sinister flame at the target to inflict a burn.

tm39.effect: Teaches Rock Tomb to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II & I: Swift)
tm39.flavor: This attack move doubles its power if the user is poisoned, burned, or paralyzed.

tm40.effect: Teaches Aerial Ace to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II: Defense Curl Gen I: Skull Bash)
tm40.flavor: Star-shaped rays are shot at opposing Pokémon. This attack never misses.

tm41.effect: Teaches Torment to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II: ThunderPunch Gen I: Softboiled)
tm41.flavor: The user assists an ally by boosting the power of that ally’s attack.

tm42.effect: Teaches Facade to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II & I: Dream Eater)
tm42.flavor: This attack move’s power is doubled if the user has been hurt by the opponent in the same turn.

tm43.effect: Teaches Flame Charge to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV & III: Secret Power Gen II: Detect Gen I: Sky Attack)
tm43.flavor: The user attacks with a swift chop. It can also break barriers, such as Light Screen and Reflect.

tm44.effect: Teaches Rest to a compatible Pokémon.
tm44.flavor: If opposing Pokémon know any move also known by the user, they are prevented from using it.

tm45.effect: Teaches Attract to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen I: Thunder Wave)
tm45.flavor: Diving on the first turn, the user floats up and attacks on the next turn.

tm46.effect: Teaches Thief to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen I: Psywave)
tm46.flavor: This attack move varies in power and type depending on the weather.

tm47.effect: Teaches Low Sweep to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV & III & II: Steel Wing Gen I: Explosion)
tm47.flavor: The user feigns crying to fluster the target, harshly lowering its Sp. Def stat.

tm48.effect: Teaches Round to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV & III: Skill Swap Gen II: Fire Punch Gen I: Rock Slide)
tm48.flavor: Boulders are hurled at the target. This also lowers the target’s Speed stat by preventing its movement.

tm49.effect: Teaches Echoed Voice to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV & III: Snatch Gen II: Fury Cutter Gen I: Tri Attack)
tm49.flavor: The user traps the target inside a harshly raging sandstorm for four to five turns.

tm50.effect: Teaches Overheat to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II: Nightmare Gen I: Substitute)
tm50.flavor: The user forcefully shoots seeds at the target two to five times in a row.

tm51.effect: Teaches Ally Switch to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV: Roost)
tm51.flavor: The user launches sharp icicles at the target two to five times in a row.

tm52.effect: Teaches Focus Blast to a compatible Pokémon.
tm52.flavor: The user bounces up high, then drops on the target on the second turn. This may also leave the target with paralysis.

tm53.effect: Teaches Energy Ball to a compatible Pokémon.
tm53.flavor: The user attacks by hurling a blob of mud at the target. This also lowers the target’s Speed stat.

tm54.effect: Teaches False Swipe to a compatible Pokémon.
tm54.flavor: The user hurls hard rocks at the target. Two to five rocks are launched in a row.

tm55.effect: Teaches Scald to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV: Brine)
tm55.flavor: If the target’s HP is half or less, this attack will hit with double the power.

tm56.effect: Teaches Fling to a compatible Pokémon.
tm56.flavor: After making its attack, the user rushes back to switch places with a party Pokémon in waiting.

tm57.effect: Teaches Charge Beam to a compatible Pokémon.
tm57.flavor: The user stores power, then attacks. If the user moves after the target, this attack’s power will be doubled.

tm58.effect: Teaches Sky Drop to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV: Endure)
tm58.flavor: If the target has already taken some damage in the same turn, this attack’s power is doubled.

tm59.effect: Teaches Incinerate to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV: Dragon Pulse)
tm59.flavor: The user flings its held item at the target to attack. This move’s power and effects depend on the item.

tm60.effect: Teaches Quash to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV: Drain Punch)
tm60.flavor: The user employs its psychic power to switch changes to its Attack and Sp. Atk stats with the target.

tm61.effect: Teaches Will-O-Wisp to a compatible Pokémon.
tm61.flavor: The user employs its psychic power to switch changes to its Defense and Sp. Def stats with the target.

tm62.effect: Teaches Acrobatics to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV: Silver Wind)
tm62.flavor: The user exchanges Speed stats with the target.

tm63.effect: Teaches Embargo to a compatible Pokémon.
tm63.flavor: An energy-draining punch. The user’s HP is restored by half the damage taken by the target.

tm64.effect: Teaches Explosion to a compatible Pokémon.
tm64.flavor: The power of this attack move is doubled if the user has been hurt by the target in the same turn.

tm65.effect: Teaches Shadow Claw to a compatible Pokémon.
tm65.flavor: The user slashes with a sharp claw made from shadows. Critical hits land more easily.

tm66.effect: Teaches Payback to a compatible Pokémon.
tm66.flavor: The user bites with electrified fangs. This may also make the target flinch or leave it with paralysis.

tm67.effect: Teaches Retaliate to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV: Recycle)
tm67.flavor: The user bites with cold-infused fangs. This may also make the target flinch or leave it frozen.

tm68.effect: Teaches Giga Impact to a compatible Pokémon.
tm68.flavor: The user bites with flame-cloaked fangs. This may also make the target flinch or leave it with a burn.

tm69.effect: Teaches Rock Polish to a compatible Pokémon.
tm69.flavor: The user tears at the target with blades formed by psychic power. Critical hits land more easily.

tm70.effect: Teaches Flash to a compatible Pokémon.
tm70.flavor: The user creates a bizarre area in which slower Pokémon get to move first for five turns.

tm71.effect: Teaches Stone Edge to a compatible Pokémon.
tm71.flavor: The user creates a bizarre area in which Pokémon’s Defense and Sp. Def stats are swapped for five turns.

tm72.effect: Teaches Volt Switch to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV: Avalanche)
tm72.flavor: The user creates a bizarre area in which Pokémon’s held items lose their effects for five turns.

tm73.effect: Teaches Thunder Wave to a compatible Pokémon.
tm73.flavor: A slashing attack with a poisonous blade that may also poison the target. Critical hits land more easily.

tm74.effect: Teaches Gyro Ball to a compatible Pokémon.
tm74.flavor: The user drenches the target in a special poisonous liquid. This move’s power is doubled if the target is poisoned.

tm75.effect: Teaches Swords Dance to a compatible Pokémon.
tm75.flavor: The user makes a swift attack on the target’s legs, which lowers the target’s Speed stat.

tm76.effect: Teaches Struggle Bug to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV: Stealth Rock)
tm76.flavor: The user attacks the target with a song. Others can join in the Round to increase the power of the attack.

tm77.effect: Teaches Psych Up to a compatible Pokémon.
tm77.flavor: This relentless attack does massive damage to a target affected by status conditions.

tm78.effect: Teaches Bulldoze to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV: Captivate)
tm78.flavor: The user nimbly strikes the target. If the user is not holding an item, this attack inflicts massive damage.

tm79.effect: Teaches Frost Breath to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV: Dark Pulse)
tm79.flavor: The user gets revenge for a fainted ally. If an ally fainted in the previous turn, this move’s power is increased.

tm80.effect: Teaches Rock Slide to a compatible Pokémon.
tm80.flavor: After making its attack, the user rushes back to switch places with a party Pokémon in waiting.

tm81.effect: Teaches X-Scissor to a compatible Pokémon.
tm81.flavor: The user strikes everything around it by stomping down on the ground. This lowers the Speed stats of those hit.

tm82.effect: Teaches Dragon Tail to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV: Sleep Talk)
tm82.flavor: The user attacks and captures opposing Pokémon using an electric net. This lowers their Speed stats.

tm83.effect: Teaches Work Up to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV: Natural Gift)
tm83.flavor: The user cuts its target with sharp shells. This may also lower the target’s Defense stat.

tm84.effect: Teaches Poison Jab to a compatible Pokémon.
tm84.flavor: The user attacks by striking the target with its hard tail. It hits the target two to five times in a row.

tm85.effect: Teaches Dream Eater to a compatible Pokémon.
tm85.flavor: The user yells as if it’s ranting about something, which lowers the Sp. Atk stats of opposing Pokémon.

tm86.effect: Teaches Grass Knot to a compatible Pokémon.
tm86.flavor: The user vanishes somewhere, then strikes the target on the next turn. This move hits even if the target protects itself.

tm87.effect: Teaches Swagger to a compatible Pokémon.
tm87.flavor: The user steals the target’s HP with a kiss. The user’s HP is restored by over half of the damage taken by the target.

tm88.effect: Teaches Pluck to a compatible Pokémon.
tm88.flavor: The user turns the ground to grass for five turns. This restores the HP of Pokémon on the ground a little every turn and powers up Grass-type moves.

tm89.effect: Teaches U-turn to a compatible Pokémon.
tm89.flavor: This protects Pokémon on the ground from status conditions and halves damage from Dragon-type moves for five turns.

tm90.effect: Teaches Substitute to a compatible Pokémon.
tm90.flavor: The user electrifies the ground for five turns, powering up Electric-type moves. Pokémon on the ground no longer fall asleep.

tm91.effect: Teaches Flash Cannon to a compatible Pokémon.
tm91.flavor: This protects Pokémon on the ground from priority moves and powers up Psychic-type moves for five turns.

tm92.effect: Teaches Trick Room to a compatible Pokémon.
tm92.flavor: The user attacks by breathing a special, hot fire. This also lowers the target’s Sp. Atk stat.

hm01.effect: Teaches Cut to a compatible Pokémon.

hm02.effect: Teaches Fly to a compatible Pokémon.

hm03.effect: Teaches Surf to a compatible Pokémon.

hm04.effect: Teaches Strength to a compatible Pokémon.

hm05.effect: Teaches Waterfall to a compatible Pokémon. (HS: Whirlpool DPP: Defog Gen III & II & I: Flash)

hm06.effect: Teaches Dive to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV & III: Rock Smash Gen II: Whirlpool)

hm07.effect: Teaches a move to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV & III & II: Waterfall)
hm07.flavor: Diving on the first turn, the user floats up and attacks on the next turn.

hm08.effect: Teaches a move to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV: Rock Climb Gen III: Dive)
hm08.flavor: A charging attack that may also leave the foe confused. It can also be used to scale rocky walls.

explorer-kit.flavor: A bag filled with convenient tools for exploring. It provides access to the Underground in the Sinnoh region.

poke-radar.effect: Use to track down rare or shiny Pokémon. 50 steps to recharge.
poke-radar.flavor: A tool that can search out Pokémon that are hiding in the tall grass. Its battery is recharged as you walk.

point-card.effect: Keeps count of Battle Points earned.
point-card.flavor: A card that lists how many Battle Points you have earned.

journal.effect: Records prior significant activities the player took.
journal.flavor: A notebook that keeps a day-to-day record of your adventure so far.

seal-case.effect: Stores Seals that can be applied to Poké Ball capsules.
seal-case.flavor: A case for storing Seals that can be applied to the exteriors of Poké Balls.

fashion-case.effect: Holds Pokémon Accessories for use in Contests.
fashion-case.flavor: A lovely case to store colorful Props for your Pokémon to wear in a musical.

pal-pad.effect: Use to record Friend Codes and check your own.
pal-pad.flavor: A convenient notepad that is used to register your friends and friend codes and keep a record of your gameplay.

town-map.effect: Use to see the overworld map.
town-map.flavor: A very convenient map that can be viewed anytime. It even shows you your present location in the region.

vs-seeker.effect: Allows rebattling of on-screen trainers. 100 steps to recharge.
vs-seeker.flavor: A device that indicates Trainers who want to battle. Its battery charges while you walk.

coin-case.effect: Holds coins for the Game Corner.
coin-case.flavor: A case for holding coins obtained at the Game Corner. It can hold up to 50,000 coins.

old-rod.effect: Used to catch Pokémon in bodies of water.
old-rod.flavor: An old and beat-up fishing rod. Use it at any body of water to fish for wild aquatic Pokémon.

good-rod.effect: Used to catch Pokémon in bodies of water.
good-rod.flavor: A new, good-quality fishing rod. Use it at any body of water to fish for wild aquatic Pokémon.

super-rod.effect: Used to catch Pokémon in bodies of water.
super-rod.flavor: An awesome, high-tech fishing rod. Use it at any body of water to fish for wild aquatic Pokémon.

sprayduck.flavor: A watering can shaped like a Psyduck. It helps promote the healthy growth of any Berries planted in good, soft soil.

poffin-case.flavor: A case for storing Poffins cooked from Berries.

bicycle.flavor: A folding Bicycle that enables a rider to get around much faster than a pair of Running Shoes will allow.

suite-key.effect: Opens a locked building in the Lakeside Resort.
suite-key.flavor: A key to one of the suites at a certain luxury hotel by a lake. For some odd reason, it often disappears.

oaks-letter.effect: Allows access to Seabreak path, Flower Paradise, and Shaymin.
oaks-letter.flavor: A letter from Professor Oak. Written in it is a request for you to go to Route 224.

lunar-wing.effect: Cures sailor’s son of nightmares in Canalave City.
lunar-wing.flavor: A feather that glows like the moon. It’s said to possess the power to dispel nightmares.

member-card.effect: Allows access to Newmoon Island and Darkrai.
member-card.flavor: A card needed for entering the inn in Canalave City. Oddly, the last date marked on it was 50 years ago.

azure-flute.effect: Allows entry into the Hall of Origin. Unreleased.
azure-flute.flavor: A flute that puts out echoing sounds that do not seem to be of this world. No one knows who made it.

contest-pass.flavor: A pass required for entering Pokémon Contests. It has a drawing of an award ribbon on its front.

griseous-orb.effect: Boosts the damage from Giratina’s Dragon-type and Ghost-type moves by 20%, and transforms it into Origin Forme.
griseous-orb.flavor: A glowing orb to be held by Giratina. It boosts the power of Dragon- and Ghost-type moves when it is held.

vs-recorder.effect: Records wireless, Wi-Fi, or Battle Frontier battles, and stores points.
vs-recorder.flavor: An amazing device that can record a battle between friends or the battles at certain special battle facilities.

gracidea.effect: Changes an unfrozen Shaymin to Sky Forme in the day.
gracidea.flavor: A flower sometimes bundled in bouquets to convey gratitude on special occasions, such as birthdays.

secret-key.effect: Gen IV: The key to Rotom’s appliance room. Gen III: The key to Cinnabar Gym.
secret-key.flavor: A high-tech key that has to be used at a specific location. It emits a special electronic signal to open a door.

apricorn-box.flavor: A handy box where you can store up to 99 of each kind of Apricorn.

berry-pots.flavor: Handy containers for cultivating Berries wherever you go.

lure-ball.effect: 3× effectiveness while fishing. Made from Blu Apricorn.
lure-ball.flavor: A Poké Ball that is good for catching Pokémon that you reel in with a rod while out fishing.

level-ball.effect: Success rate based off of fraction target Pokémon is of user’s Pokémon. Made from Red Apricorn.
level-ball.flavor: A Poké Ball that makes it easier to catch Pokémon that are at a lower level than your own Pokémon.

moon-ball.effect: 4× effectiveness on familes of Pokémon with a Moon Stone evolution. Made from Ylw Apricorn.
moon-ball.flavor: A Poké Ball that will make it easier to catch Pokémon that can evolve using a Moon Stone.

heavy-ball.effect: Has flat bonus or penalty to catch rate depending on weight class of target. Made from Blk Apricorn.
heavy-ball.flavor: A Poké Ball that is better than usual at catching very heavy Pokémon.

fast-ball.effect: 4× effectiveness on Pokémon with 100 or greater base speed. (Gen II: Roaming or Fleeing Pokémon). Made from Wht Apricorn.
fast-ball.flavor: A Poké Ball that makes it easier to catch Pokémon that are usually very quick to run away.

friend-ball.effect: Caught Pokémon start with 200 happiness. Made from Grn Apricorn.
friend-ball.flavor: A strange Poké Ball that will make the wild Pokémon caught with it more friendly toward you immediately.

love-ball.effect: 8× effectiveness on opposite sex, same species targets of the Active Pokémon. Made from Pnk Apricorn.
love-ball.flavor: A Poké Ball that works best when catching a Pokémon that is of the opposite gender of your Pokémon.

park-ball.effect: Catches Pokémon in the Pal Park every time.
park-ball.flavor: A special Poké Ball for the Pal Park.

sport-ball.effect: Tries to catch a Pokémon in the Bug-Catching contest in National Park. (Gen II: Park Ball)
sport-ball.flavor: A special Poké Ball that was used during the Bug-Catching Contest in the Johto region.

red-apricorn.effect: Used to make a Level Ball.
red-apricorn.flavor: A red Apricorn. It assails your nostrils.

blue-apricorn.effect: Used to make a Lure Ball.
blue-apricorn.flavor: A blue Apricorn. It smells a bit like grass.

yellow-apricorn.effect: Used to make a Moon Ball.
yellow-apricorn.flavor: A yellow Apricorn. It has an invigorating scent.

green-apricorn.effect: Used to make a Friend Ball.
green-apricorn.flavor: A green Apricorn. It has a mysterious, aromatic scent.

pink-apricorn.effect: Used to make a Love Ball.
pink-apricorn.flavor: A pink Apricorn. It has a nice, sweet scent.

white-apricorn.effect: Used to make a Fast Ball.
white-apricorn.flavor: A white Apricorn. It doesn’t smell like anything.

black-apricorn.effect: Used to make a Heavy Ball.
black-apricorn.flavor: A black Apricorn. It has a scent beyond one’s experience.

dowsing-machine.effect: Use to find hidden items on the field. AKA Itemfinder.
dowsing-machine.flavor: A high-tech machine that shows the location of invisible items. It’s attached to the head when in use.

rage-candy-bar.effect: HS: Traded for TM64. Gen II & Gen V: Acts as a Potion.
rage-candy-bar.flavor: Mahogany Town’s famous candy. It can be used once to heal all the status conditions of a Pokémon.

enigma-stone.effect: S: Summons Latias H: Summons Latios.
enigma-stone.flavor: A crystal ball that was excavated from the ground. It’s a very beautiful stone that is covered with bits of rock and earth.

unown-report.effect: Keeps track of Unown types caught.
unown-report.flavor: A report of all the discovered kinds of Unown.

blue-card.effect: Keeps track of points from Buena’s show.
blue-card.flavor: A card to save points for the Buena’s Password show.

gb-sounds.effect: Use to listen to GameBoy era audio.
gb-sounds.flavor: A music player that allows you to listen to nostalgic songs. It’s operated with the flip of a single switch.

data-card-01.effect: Records the number of times the trainer has come in first place overall in the Pokéathlon.
data-card-01.flavor: It holds Pokéathlon records. It is a card that shows how many victories you have had in the Pokéathlon.

data-card-02.effect: Records the number of times the trainer has come in last place overall in the Pokéathlon.
data-card-02.flavor: It holds Pokéathlon records. It is a card that shows how many losses you have had in the Pokéathlon.

data-card-03.effect: Records the number of times the trainer’s Pokémon have dashed in the Pokéathlon.
data-card-03.flavor: It holds Pokéathlon records. It is a card that shows how many times your Pokémon dashed in the Pokéathlon.

data-card-04.effect: Records the number of times the trainer’s Pokémon have jumped in the Pokéathlon.
data-card-04.flavor: It holds Pokéathlon records. It is a card that shows how many times your Pokémon jumped in the Pokéathlon.

data-card-05.effect: Records the number of times the trainer has come in first in the Pokéathlon Hurdle Dash.
data-card-05.flavor: It holds Pokéathlon records. It is a card that shows how many times you won the Pokéathlon’s Hurdle Dash.

data-card-06.effect: Records the number of times the trainer has come in first in the Pokéathlon Relay Run.
data-card-06.flavor: It holds Pokéathlon records. It is a card that shows how many times you won the Pokéathlon’s Relay Run.

data-card-07.effect: Records the number of times the trainer has come in first in the Pokéathlon Pennant Capture.
data-card-07.flavor: It holds Pokéathlon records. It is a card that shows how many times you won the Pokéathlon’s Pennant Capture.

data-card-08.effect: Records the number of times the trainer has come in first in the Pokéathlon Block Smash.
data-card-08.flavor: It holds Pokéathlon records. It is a card that shows how many times you won the Pokéathlon’s Block Smash.

data-card-09.effect: Records the number of times the trainer has come in first in the Pokéathlon Disc Catch.
data-card-09.flavor: It holds Pokéathlon records. It is a card that shows how many times you won the Pokéathlon’s Disc Catch.

data-card-10.effect: Records the number of times the trainer has come in first in the Pokéathlon Snow Throw.
data-card-10.flavor: It holds Pokéathlon records. It is a card that shows how many times you won the Pokéathlon’s Snow Throw.

data-card-11.effect: Records the number of points the trainer has earned in the Pokéathlon.
data-card-11.flavor: It holds Pokéathlon records. It is a card that shows how many points your Pokémon scored in the Pokéathlon.

data-card-12.effect: Records the number of times the trainer’s Pokémon have messed up in the Pokéathlon.
data-card-12.flavor: It holds Pokéathlon records. It is a card that shows how many times your Pokémon failed in the Pokéathlon.

data-card-13.effect: Records the number of times the trainer’s Pokémon have defeated themselves in the Pokéathlon.
data-card-13.flavor: It holds Pokéathlon records. It is a card that shows how many times your Pokémon impeded themselves.

data-card-14.effect: Records the number of times the trainer’s Pokémon have tackled in the Pokéathlon.
data-card-14.flavor: It holds Pokéathlon records. It is a card that shows how many times your Pokémon tackled in the Pokéathlon.

data-card-15.effect: Records the number of times the trainer’s Pokémon have fallen in the Pokéathlon.
data-card-15.flavor: It holds Pokéathlon records. It is a card that shows how many times your Pokémon fell down in the Pokéathlon.

data-card-16.effect: Records the number of times the trainer has come in first in the Pokéathlon Ring Drop.
data-card-16.flavor: It holds Pokéathlon records. It is a card that shows how many times you won the Pokéathlon’s Ring Drop.

data-card-17.effect: Records the number of times the trainer has come in first in the Pokéathlon Lamp Jump.
data-card-17.flavor: It holds Pokéathlon records. It is a card that shows how many times you won the Pokéathlon’s Lamp Jump.

data-card-18.effect: Records the number of times the trainer has come in first in the Pokéathlon Circle Push.
data-card-18.flavor: It holds Pokéathlon records. It is a card that shows how many times you won the Pokéathlon’s Circle Push.

data-card-19.effect: Records the number of times the trainer has come in first place overall in the Pokéathlon over wirelss.
data-card-19.flavor: It holds Pokéathlon records. It is a card that shows how many Link Pokéathlon wins you have had.

data-card-20.effect: Records the number of times the trainer has come in last place overall in the Pokéathlon over wireless.
data-card-20.flavor: It holds Pokéathlon records. It is a card that shows how many Link Pokéathlon losses you have had.

data-card-21.effect: Records the number of times the trainer has come in first across all Pokéathlon events.
data-card-21.flavor: It holds Pokéathlon records. It is a card that shows how many times you have won a Pokéathlon event.

data-card-22.effect: Records the number of times the trainer has come in last across all Pokéathlon events.
data-card-22.flavor: It holds Pokéathlon records. It is a card that shows how many times you have lost a Pokéathlon event.

data-card-23.effect: Records the number of times the trainer has switched Pokémon in the Pokéathlon.
data-card-23.flavor: It holds Pokéathlon records. It is a card that shows how many times you switched Pokémon in the Pokéathlon.

data-card-24.effect: Records the number of times the trainer has come in first in the Pokéathlon Goal Roll.
data-card-24.flavor: It holds Pokéathlon records. It is a card that shows how many times you won the Pokéathlon’s Goal Roll.

data-card-25.effect: Records the number of times the trainer’s Pokémon received prizes in the Pokéathlon.
data-card-25.flavor: It holds Pokéathlon records. It is a card that shows how many times your Pokémon won individual prizes.

data-card-26.effect: Records the number of times the trainer has instructed Pokémon in the Pokéathlon.
data-card-26.flavor: It holds Pokéathlon records. It is a card that shows how many times you instructed your Pokémon.

data-card-27.effect: Records the total time spent in the Pokéathlon.
data-card-27.flavor: It holds Pokéathlon records. It is a card that shows how much time you spent participating in the Pokéathlon.

orange-mail.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
orange-mail.flavor: A piece of MAIL featuring a cute ZIGZAGOON print. It is to be held by a POKéMON.

harbor-mail.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
harbor-mail.flavor: A piece of MAIL featuring a cute WINGULL print. It is to be held by a POKéMON.

glitter-mail.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
glitter-mail.flavor: A piece of MAIL featuring a cute PIKACHU print. It is to be held by a POKéMON.

mech-mail.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
mech-mail.flavor: A piece of MAIL featuring a cute MAGNEMITE print. It is to be held by a POKéMON.

wood-mail.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
wood-mail.flavor: A piece of MAIL featuring a cute SLAKOTH print. It is to be held by a POKéMON.

wave-mail.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
wave-mail.flavor: A piece of MAIL featuring a cute WAILMER print. It is to be held by a POKéMON.

bead-mail.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
bead-mail.flavor: A piece of MAIL to be held by a POKéMON. It will bear the print of the POKéMON holding it.

shadow-mail.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
shadow-mail.flavor: A piece of MAIL featuring a cute DUSKULL print. It is to be held by a POKéMON.

tropic-mail.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
tropic-mail.flavor: A piece of MAIL featuring a cute BELLOSSOM print. It is to be held by a POKéMON.

dream-mail.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
dream-mail.flavor: A piece of MAIL to be held by a POKéMON. It will bear the print of the POKéMON holding it.

fab-mail.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
fab-mail.flavor: A piece of MAIL featuring a gorgeous, extravagant print. It is to be held by a POKéMON.

retro-mail.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
retro-mail.flavor: A piece of MAIL featuring a print of three cute POKéMON. It is to be held by a POKéMON.

mach-bike.effect: Faster than the Acro Bike, and can ride up sandy slopes and across cracked floors.
mach-bike.flavor: This folding bike more than doubles your movement speed.

acro-bike.effect: More maneuverable than the Mach Bike, and allows hopping along rails.
acro-bike.flavor: This folding bike allows you to perform actions such as wheelies and bunny hops.

wailmer-pail.flavor: This is a tool for watering Berries you planted to make them grow more quickly.

soot-sack.flavor: A sack used to gather and hold volcanic ash.

pokeblock-case.flavor: A case for holding POKéBLOCKS made with a BERRY BLENDER. It releases one POKéBLOCK when shaken.

eon-ticket.effect: Provides access to Southern Island and Latias or Latios.
eon-ticket.flavor: The ticket required for sailing on a ferry to the Southern Island. Does Norman, the Petalburg Gym Leader, know a secret?!

go-goggles.flavor: Nifty goggles to protect eyes from desert sandstorms.

devon-scope.flavor: A special device made by Devon Corporation that signals the presence of any unseen Pokémon.

fame-checker.flavor: A device that enables you to recall what you’ve heard and seen about famous people.

berry-pouch.flavor: A pouch for carrying BERRIES. It is attached to the BAG’s compartment for important items.

teachy-tv.flavor: A television set that is tuned to a program with useful tips for novice TRAINERS.

mysticticket.effect: Allows access to Navel Rock and Lugia or Ho-oh.
mysticticket.flavor: A ticket required to board the ship to NAVEL ROCK. It glows with a mystic light.

auroraticket.effect: Allows access to Birth Island and Deoxys.
auroraticket.flavor: A ticket required to board the ship to BIRTH ISLAND. It glows beautifully.

powder-jar.effect: Stores Berry Powder made using a Berry Crusher.
powder-jar.flavor: A jar for storing BERRY POWDER made using a BERRY CRUSHER.

old-sea-map.effect: Allows access to Faraway Island and Mew. Unreleased outside of Japan.
old-sea-map.flavor: A faded sea chart that shows the way to a certain island.

douse-drive.effect: Grants Genesect a blue, Water-type Techno Blast.
douse-drive.flavor: A cassette to be held by Genesect. It changes Genesect’s Techno Blast move so it becomes Water type.

shock-drive.effect: Grants Genesect a yellow, Electric-type Techno Blast.
shock-drive.flavor: A cassette to be held by Genesect. It changes Genesect’s Techno Blast move so it becomes Electric type.

burn-drive.effect: Grants Genesect a red, Fire-type Techno Blast.
burn-drive.flavor: A cassette to be held by Genesect. It changes Genesect’s Techno Blast move so it becomes Fire type.

chill-drive.effect: Grants Genesect a white, Ice-type Techno Blast.
chill-drive.flavor: A cassette to be held by Genesect. It changes Genesect’s Techno Blast move so it becomes Ice type.

sweet-heart.flavor: A piece of cloyingly sweet chocolate. It can be used to restore 20 HP to a single Pokémon.

greet-mail.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
greet-mail.flavor: Stationery designed to be easy to use when writing someone for the first time. Have a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

favored-mail.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
favored-mail.flavor: Stationery designed to make it easier to write about your favorite things. Have a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

rsvp-mail.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
rsvp-mail.flavor: Stationery designed to allow you to extend an invitation to the recipient. Have a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

thanks-mail.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
thanks-mail.flavor: Stationery designed to make it easy for you to express thanks. Have a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

inquiry-mail.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
inquiry-mail.flavor: Stationery designed to make it easier to pose a question about something. Have a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

like-mail.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
like-mail.flavor: Stationery designed to allow you to recommend something to the reader. Have a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

reply-mail.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
reply-mail.flavor: Stationery designed to make it easy to respond to a letter you received. Have a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

bridge-mail-s.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
bridge-mail-s.flavor: Stationery featuring an illustration of a bridge that seems to pierce the sky. Have a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

bridge-mail-d.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
bridge-mail-d.flavor: Stationery featuring an illustration of a bright-red drawbridge. Have a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

bridge-mail-t.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
bridge-mail-t.flavor: Stationery featuring an illustration of a high-tension steel suspension bridge. Have a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

bridge-mail-v.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
bridge-mail-v.flavor: Stationery featuring an illustration of a vintage-looking brick bridge. Have a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

bridge-mail-m.effect: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
bridge-mail-m.flavor: Stationery featuring an illustration of a majestically arched bridge. Have a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

prism-scale.effect: Traded on a Feebas: Holder evolves into Milotic.
prism-scale.flavor: A mysterious scale that causes a certain Pokémon to evolve. It shines in rainbow colors.

eviolite.effect: Held: Holder has 1.5× Defense and Special Defense, as long as it’s not fully evolved.
eviolite.flavor: A mysterious evolutionary lump. When held by a Pokémon that can still evolve, it raises both Defense and Sp. Def.

float-stone.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. This very light stone reduces the weight of a Pokémon when held.

rocky-helmet.effect: Held: When the holder is hit by a contact move, the attacking Pokémon takes 1/6 its max HP in damage.
rocky-helmet.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. If the holder is hit, the attacker will also be damaged upon contact.

air-balloon.effect: Held: Grants immunity to Ground-type moves, Spikes, and Toxic Spikes. Consumed when the holder takes damage from a move.
air-balloon.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. The holder will float in the air until hit. Once the holder is hit, this item will burst.

red-card.effect: Held: When the holder takes damage from a move, the opponent switches out for another random party Pokémon. Consumed after use.
red-card.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. When the holder is hit by an attack, the attacker is removed from battle.

ring-target.effect: Held: Negates the holder’s type immunities. Ability immunities are not removed.
ring-target.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. Moves that normally have no effect will land on a Pokémon holding it.

binding-band.effect: Held: Doubles the per-turn damage of multi-turn trapping moves.
binding-band.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a band that increases the power of binding moves used by the holder.

absorb-bulb.effect: Held: Raises the holder’s Special Attack by one stage when it takes Water-type damage.
absorb-bulb.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It boosts Sp. Atk if the holder is hit with a Water-type attack. It can only be used once.

cell-battery.effect: Held: Raises the holder’s Attack by one stage when it takes Electric-type damage.
cell-battery.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It boosts Attack if the holder is hit with an Electric-type attack. It can only be used once.

eject-button.effect: Held: When the holder takes damage from a move, it switches out for a party Pokémon of the Trainer’s choice.
eject-button.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. If the holder is hit by an attack, it will be switched out of battle.

fire-gem.effect: Held: When the holder uses a damaging fire-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
fire-gem.flavor: A gem with an essence of fire. When held, it strengthens the power of a Fire-type move one time.

water-gem.effect: Held: When the holder uses a damaging water-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
water-gem.flavor: A gem with an essence of water. When held, it strengthens the power of a Water-type move one time.

electric-gem.effect: Held: When the holder uses a damaging electric-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
electric-gem.flavor: A gem with an essence of electricity. When held, it strengthens the power of an Electric-type move one time.

grass-gem.effect: Held: When the holder uses a damaging grass-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
grass-gem.flavor: A gem with an essence of nature. When held, it strengthens the power of a Grass-type move one time.

ice-gem.effect: Held: When the holder uses a damaging ice-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
ice-gem.flavor: A gem with an essence of ice. When held, it strengthens the power of an Ice-type move one time.

fighting-gem.effect: Held: When the holder uses a damaging fighting-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
fighting-gem.flavor: A gem with an essence of combat. When held, it strengthens the power of a Fighting-type move one time.

poison-gem.effect: Held: When the holder uses a damaging poison-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
poison-gem.flavor: A gem with an essence of poison. When held, it strengthens the power of a Poison-type move one time.

ground-gem.effect: Held: When the holder uses a damaging ground-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
ground-gem.flavor: A gem with an essence of land. When held, it strengthens the power of a Ground-type move one time.

flying-gem.effect: Held: When the holder uses a damaging flying-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
flying-gem.flavor: A gem with an essence of air. When held, it strengthens the power of a Flying-type move one time.

psychic-gem.effect: Held: When the holder uses a damaging psychic-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
psychic-gem.flavor: A gem with an essence of the mind. When held, it strengthens the power of a Psychic-type move one time.

bug-gem.effect: Held: When the holder uses a damaging bug-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
bug-gem.flavor: A gem with an insect-like essence. When held, it strengthens the power of a Bug-type move one time.

rock-gem.effect: Held: When the holder uses a damaging rock-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
rock-gem.flavor: A gem with an essence of rock. When held, it strengthens the power of a Rock-type move one time.

ghost-gem.effect: Held: When the holder uses a damaging ghost-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
ghost-gem.flavor: A gem with a spectral essence. When held, it strengthens the power of a Ghost-type move one time.

dark-gem.effect: Held: When the holder uses a damaging dark-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
dark-gem.flavor: A gem with an essence of darkness. When held, it strengthens the power of a Dark-type move one time.

steel-gem.effect: Held: When the holder uses a damaging steel-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
steel-gem.flavor: A gem with an essence of steel. When held, it strengthens the power of a Steel-type move one time.

health-wing.flavor: An item for use on a Pokémon. It slightly increases the HP base points of a single Pokémon.

muscle-wing.flavor: An item for use on a Pokémon. It slightly increases the Attack base points of a single Pokémon.

resist-wing.flavor: An item for use on a Pokémon. It slightly increases the Defense base points of a single Pokémon.

genius-wing.flavor: An item for use on a Pokémon. It slightly increases the Sp. Atk base points of a single Pokémon.

clever-wing.flavor: An item for use on a Pokémon. It slightly increases the Sp. Def base points of a single Pokémon.

swift-wing.flavor: An item for use on a Pokémon. It slightly increases the Speed base points of a single Pokémon.

pretty-wing.flavor: Though this feather is beautiful, it’s just a regular feather and has no effect.

cover-fossil.effect: Can be revived into a tirtouga.
cover-fossil.flavor: A fossil from a prehistoric Pokémon that once lived in the sea. It appears as though it could be part of its back.

plume-fossil.effect: Can be revived into an archen.
plume-fossil.flavor: A fossil from a prehistoric Pokémon that once lived in the sky. It looks as if it could come from part of its wing.

liberty-pass.effect: Allows access to Liberty Garden and Victini.
liberty-pass.flavor: A special pass to go to Liberty Garden. Board the ship in Castelia City.

pass-orb.flavor: A mysterious orb containing the power of the Unova region, to be used when generating Pass Power.

dream-ball.effect: Catches Pokémon found in the Dream World.
dream-ball.flavor: A somewhat different Poké Ball that makes it easier to catch wild Pokémon while they’re asleep.

poke-toy.flavor: A toy that attracts the attention of a Pokémon. It guarantees escape from any battle with wild Pokémon.

prop-case.effect: Stores props for the Pokémon Musical.
prop-case.flavor: A lovely case to store colorful Props for your Pokémon to wear in a musical.

balm-mushroom.effect: Sell to Hungry Maid for 25000 Pokédollars.
balm-mushroom.flavor: A rare mushroom that gives off a nice fragrance. It can be sold at a high price to shops.

big-nugget.effect: Sell to Ore Collector for 30000 Pokédollars.
big-nugget.flavor: A big nugget of pure gold that gives off a lustrous gleam. It can be sold at a high price to shops.

pearl-string.effect: Sell to Ore Collector for 25000 Pokédollars.
pearl-string.flavor: Very large pearls that sparkle in a pretty silver color. They can be sold at a high price to shops.

comet-shard.effect: Sell to Ore Collector for 60000 Pokédollars.
comet-shard.flavor: A shard that fell to the ground when a comet approached. It can be sold at a high price to shops.

relic-copper.effect: Sell to Villa Owner for 1000 Pokédollars.
relic-copper.flavor: A copper coin used by an ancient civilization about three thousand years ago.

relic-silver.flavor: A silver coin used by an ancient civilization about three thousand years ago.

relic-gold.flavor: A gold coin used by an ancient civilization about three thousand years ago.

relic-vase.flavor: A fragile vase made by an ancient civilization about three thousand years ago.

relic-band.effect: Sell to Villa Owner for 100000 Pokédollars.
relic-band.flavor: A heavy bracelet made by an ancient civilization about three thousand years ago.

relic-statue.flavor: A stone figurine made by an ancient civilization about three thousand years ago.

relic-crown.effect: Sell to Villa Owner for 300000 Pokédollars.
