ability-urge.effect: Forcibly activates a friendly Pokémon’s ability.
ability-urge.flavor: When used, it activates the Ability of an ally Pokémon.

item-drop.effect: Forces a friendly Pokémon to drop its held item.
item-drop.flavor: When used, it causes an ally Pokémon to drop a held item.

item-urge.effect: Forcibly activates a friendly Pokémon’s held item.
item-urge.flavor: When used, it causes an ally Pokémon to use its held item.

reset-urge.effect: Resets a friendly Pokémon’s stat changes.
reset-urge.flavor: When used, it restores any stat changes of an ally Pokémon.

dire-hit-3.effect: Raises critical hit rate by three stages in battle.  Wonder Launcher only.
dire-hit-3.flavor: It can be used many times to greatly raise a Pokémon’s critical-hit ratio. It wears off if the Pokémon is withdrawn.

tm93.effect: Teaches Wild Charge to a compatible Pokémon.
tm93.flavor: The user’s body generates an eerie impulse. Exposing the target to it harshly lowers the target’s Sp. Atk stat.

tm94.effect: Teaches Rock Smash to a compatible Pokémon.
tm94.flavor: A restrained attack that prevents the target from fainting. The target is left with at least 1 HP.

tm95.effect: Teaches Snarl to a compatible Pokémon.
tm95.flavor: The user attacks with a blade of air that slices even the sky. This may also make the target flinch.

xtransceiver.flavor: A high-tech transceiver with a camera function. It allows up to four-way calls.

dragon-gem.effect: Held: When the holder uses a damaging dragon-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
dragon-gem.flavor: A gem with a draconic essence. When held, it strengthens the power of a Dragon-type move one time.

normal-gem.effect: Held: When the holder uses a damaging normal-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
normal-gem.flavor: A gem with an ordinary essence. When held, it strengthens the power of a Normal-type move one time.

medal-box.effect: Holds medals recieved in the medal rally.
medal-box.flavor: A box-shaped machine that stores Medals and Medal information.

dna-splicers.effect: Fuses Kyurem with Reshiram or Zekrom, or splits them apart again.
dna-splicers.flavor: A splicer that fuses Kyurem and a certain Pokémon. They are said to have been one in the beginning.

permit.effect: Grants access to the Nature Preserve.
permit.flavor: A permit that is needed to enter the Nature Preserve. Not many know about it.

oval-charm.effect: Doubles the chance of two Pokémon producing an egg at the daycare every 255 steps.
oval-charm.flavor: An oval charm said to increase the chance of Pokémon Eggs being found at the Nursery.

shiny-charm.effect: Raises the chance of finding a shiny Pokémon.
shiny-charm.flavor: A shiny charm said to increase the chance of finding a Shiny Pokémon in the wild.

reveal-glass.effect: Switches Tornadus, Thundurus, and Landorus between Incarnate and Therian Forme.
reveal-glass.flavor: A looking glass that reveals the truth. It’s a mysterious glass that returns a Pokémon to its original shape.

weakness-policy.effect: Held: When the holder is hit by a super effective move, its Attack and Special Attack raise by two stages.
weakness-policy.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. Attack and Sp. Atk sharply increase if the holder is hit with a move it’s weak to.

assault-vest.effect: Raises the holder’s Special Defense to 1.5×.  Prevents the holder from selecting a status move.
assault-vest.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. This offensive vest raises Sp. Def but prevents the use of status moves.

pixie-plate.effect: Held: Fairy-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Fairy.
pixie-plate.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet that boosts the power of Fairy-type moves.

ability-capsule.effect: Switches a Pokémon between its two possible (non-Hidden) Abilities.
ability-capsule.flavor: A capsule that allows a Pokémon with two Abilities to switch between these Abilities when it is used.

whipped-dream.effect: Traded on a Swirlix: Holder evolves into Slurpuff.
whipped-dream.flavor: A soft and sweet treat made of fluffy, puffy, whipped, and whirled cream. It’s loved by a certain Pokémon.

sachet.effect: Traded on a Spritzee: Holder evolves into Aromatisse.
sachet.flavor: A sachet filled with fragrant perfumes that are just slightly too overwhelming. Yet it’s loved by a certain Pokémon.

luminous-moss.effect: Held: If the holder is hit by a damaging Water move, raises its Special Defense by one stage.
luminous-moss.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It boosts Sp. Def if the holder is hit with a Water-type attack. It can only be used once.

snowball.effect: Held: If the holder is hit by a damaging Ice move, raises its Attack by one stage.
snowball.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It boosts Attack if the holder is hit with an Ice-type attack. It can only be used once.

safety-goggles.effect: Held: Prevents damage from powder moves and the damage from Hail and Sandstorm.
safety-goggles.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. These goggles protect the holder from both weather-related damage and powder.

rich-mulch.effect: Increases the total number of Berries by 2.
rich-mulch.flavor: A fertilizer for growing Berries. However, it appears to be unsuitable for the soil in the Hoenn region and has no effect.

surprise-mulch.effect: Increases the chance of Berry mutation.
surprise-mulch.flavor: A fertilizer for growing Berries. However, it appears to be unsuitable for the soil in the Hoenn region and has no effect.

boost-mulch.effect: Causes soil to dry out in 4 hours.
boost-mulch.flavor: A fertilizer for growing Berries. However, it appears to be unsuitable for the soil in the Hoenn region and has no effect.

amaze-mulch.effect: Increases the total number of Berries by 2, increases the chance of Berry mutation, and causes soil to dry out in 4 hours.
amaze-mulch.flavor: A fertilizer for growing Berries. However, it appears to be unsuitable for the soil in the Hoenn region and has no effect.

discount-coupon.flavor: This special coupon allows you to buy items at a discount when you are shopping at a boutique.

strange-souvenir.flavor: An ornament depicting a mysterious Pokémon that has been venerated as a guardian deity for an extremely long time in the Alola region.

lumiose-galette.effect: Cures all major status ailments and confusion.
lumiose-galette.flavor: A popular treat in Lumiose City. It can be used once to heal all the status conditions of a Pokémon.

jaw-fossil.effect: Can be revived into a Tyrunt.
jaw-fossil.flavor: A fossil from a prehistoric Pokémon that once lived on the land. It looks as if it could be a piece of a large jaw.

sail-fossil.effect: Can be revived into an Amaura.
sail-fossil.flavor: A fossil from a prehistoric Pokémon that once lived on the land. It looks like the impression from a skin sail.

fairy-gem.effect: Held: When the holder uses a damaging Fairy move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
fairy-gem.flavor: A gem with an essence of the fey. When held, it strengthens the power of a Fairy-type move one time.

adventure-rules.flavor: A compact device that automatically collects and records advice when a Trainer is out on an adventure.

holo-caster.effect: Displays cutscene conversations as the plot advances.
holo-caster.flavor: A device that allows users to receive and view hologram clips at any time. It’s also used to chat with others.

lens-case.effect: Allows the player to change their eye color.
lens-case.flavor: A rather chic-looking case for carrying contact lenses.

mega-ring.effect: Allows the player’s Pokémon to Mega Evolve.
mega-ring.flavor: This ring contains an untold power that somehow enables Pokémon carrying Mega Stones to Mega Evolve in battle.

roller-skates.effect: Allows the player to move quickly and off the grid.
roller-skates.flavor: Attaches roller skates to the bottom of your shoes, allowing you to glide quickly around and perform tricks.

sprinklotad.flavor: A watering can shaped like a Lotad. It helps promote the healthy growth of any Berries planted in good, soft soil.

tmv-pass.flavor: A commuter pass that allows the holder to ride the TMV between Lumiose City and Kiloude City at any time.

tm96.flavor: The user stabs the target with a sharp horn. This attack never misses.

tm97.flavor: The user swings its body around violently to inflict damage on everything in its vicinity.

tm98.flavor: Driven by frustration, the user attacks the target. If the user’s previous move has failed, the power of this move doubles.

tm99.flavor: The user swings its tough tail wildly and attacks opposing Pokémon. This also lowers their Attack stats.

tm100.flavor: The user tells the target a secret, and the target loses its ability to concentrate. This lowers the target’s Sp. Atk stat.

forage-bag.flavor: A bag to carry ingredients gathered during Mallow’s trial in the jungle.

fishing-rod.effect: Allows the player to fish for Pokémon.
fishing-rod.flavor: A fishing rod made by Captain Lana. Cast a line over piles of underwater rocks to fish for wild aquatic Pokémon.

festival-ticket.effect: Hosts a mission in Festival Plaza.
festival-ticket.flavor: A ticket that allows you to host a mission in Festival Plaza.

adrenaline-orb.effect: Makes wild Pokémon more likely to summon allies.  Held: increases the holder’s Speed by one stage when affected by Intimidate.
adrenaline-orb.flavor: If held by a Pokémon, it boosts Speed when intimidated.

zygarde-cube.effect: Contains collected Zygarde cells/cores.  Can teach Zygarde moves.
zygarde-cube.flavor: An item in which Zygarde Cores and Cells are gathered. You can also use it to change Zygarde’s forms.

ice-stone.effect: Evolves an Alola Sandshrew into Alola Sandslash or an Alola Vulpix into Alola Ninetales.
ice-stone.flavor: A peculiar stone that can make certain species of Pokémon evolve. It has an unmistakable snowflake pattern.

beast-ball.effect: Tries to catch a wild Pokémon.  Success rate is 5× for Ultra Beasts and 0.1× for all other Pokémon.
beast-ball.flavor: A somewhat different Poké Ball that has a low success rate for catching a Pokémon.

big-malasada.effect: Cures major status ailments and confusion.
big-malasada.flavor: The Alola region’s local specialty—fried bread. It can be used once to heal all the status conditions of a Pokémon.

red-nectar.flavor: A flower nectar obtained at Ula’ula Meadow. It changes the form of certain species of Pokémon.

yellow-nectar.flavor: A flower nectar obtained at Melemele Meadow. It changes the form of certain species of Pokémon.

pink-nectar.flavor: A flower nectar obtained from the flowering shrubs on Royal Avenue. It changes the form of certain species of Pokémon.

purple-nectar.flavor: A flower nectar obtained at Poni Meadow. It changes the form of certain species of Pokémon.

terrain-extender.effect: Held: Extends the holder’s Terrain effects to 8 turns.
terrain-extender.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It extends the duration of the terrain caused by the holder’s move or Ability.

protective-pads.effect: Held: Prevents side effects of contact moves used on the holder.
protective-pads.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. These pads protect the holder from effects caused by making direct contact with the target.

electric-seed.effect: Held: Consumed on Electric Terrain and raises the holder’s Defense by one stage.
electric-seed.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It boosts Defense on Electric Terrain. It can only be used once.

psychic-seed.effect: Held: Consumed on Psychic Terrain and raises the holder’s Special Defense by one stage.
psychic-seed.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It boosts Sp. Def on Psychic Terrain. It can only be used once.

misty-seed.effect: Held: Consumed on Misty Terrain and raises the holder’s Special Defense by one stage.
misty-seed.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It boosts Sp. Def on Misty Terrain. It can only be used once.

grassy-seed.effect: Held: Consumed on Grassy Terrain and raises the holder’s Defense by one stage.
grassy-seed.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It boosts Defense on Grassy Terrain. It can only be used once.

fighting-memory.flavor: A memory disc that contains Fighting-type data. It changes the type of the holder if held by a certain species of Pokémon.

golden-nanab-berry.flavor: A Berry that calms wild Pokémon you’re trying to catch drastically when given to them.

silver-pinap-berry.flavor: A Berry that makes you more likely to get an item when given to Pokémon you’re trying to catch.

golden-pinap-berry.flavor: A Berry that makes you drastically more likely to get an item when given to Pokémon you’re trying to catch.

stretchy-spring.flavor: A thin small spring. It’s totally stretched out, and what it’s used for is unknown.

chalky-stone.flavor: A small whitish stone picked up at the edge of the road.

marble.flavor: A round glass marble. You can see colored glass inside the transparent marble.

lone-earring.flavor: A single earring that somebody dropped.

beach-glass.flavor: A piece of colored glass. Waves have rounded its edges. It’s slightly rough to the touch.

gold-leaf.flavor: A mysterious gold leaf. No trees with this leaf have been found yet.

silver-leaf.flavor: A mysterious silver leaf. No trees with this leaf have been found yet.

polished-mud-ball.flavor: A ball made of mud. It had been polished for such a long time that the surface is very shiny.

tropical-shell.flavor: A beautiful white shell that may have drifted from a sea in a warm region.

leaf-letter--pikachu.flavor: A letter written on a leaf. Something is written using Pikachu’s footprints.

leaf-letter--eevee.flavor: A letter written on a leaf. Something is written using Eevee’s footprints.

small-bouquet.flavor: A small bouquet that was made with heart and soul for a single Trainer.

lure.flavor: A glass bottle of perfume that makes rare Pokémon more likely to appear.

super-lure.flavor: A glass bottle of perfume that makes rare Pokémon more likely to appear. It lasts longer than a Lure.

max-lure.flavor: A glass bottle of perfume that makes rare Pokémon more likely to appear. It lasts longer than a Super Lure.

pewter-crunchies.flavor: Pewter City’s famous crunchy snack. They can be used to heal all status conditions of a single Pokémon.

pokemon-box-link.flavor: A device that allows you to access the Pokémon storage system. There are some places where it won’t work.

fishing-rod--galar.flavor: Use it by the water to fish up various kinds of Pokémon.

rotom-bike.flavor: This bike can be combined with the Pokémon Rotom to use a turbo boost.

sausages.flavor: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. These sausages should be boiled before eating.

bobs-food-tin.flavor: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. The tinned food that Bob sells is curiously popular in Galar.

bachs-food-tin.flavor: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. The tinned food that Bach sells is curiously popular in Galar.

tin-of-beans.flavor: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. When simmered, the beans suffuse a dish with their mild flavor.

bread.flavor: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. An indispensable tool for mopping up that extra curry left on your plate.

pasta.flavor: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. These flour noodles make for a surprisingly good match with curry.

mixed-mushrooms.flavor: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. The chewy texture of the mushrooms transforms any dish they’re added to.

smoke-poke-tail.flavor: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. When a Slowpoke’s tail falls off, it grows back quickly.

large-leek.flavor: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. Whether this is the kind of vegetable stalk that Farfetch’d like is unknown.

fancy-apple.flavor: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. An apple chosen above others. It has spectacular form and a brilliant sheen.

brittle-bones.flavor: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. The taste boiled off from the bones is sure to give a dish a light umami taste.

pack-of-potatoes.flavor: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. Potatoes help mellow out a spicy taste, to make a dish more mild.

pungent-root.flavor: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. Though carelessly picked, these herbs can add a fragrant accent to a dish.

salad-mix.flavor: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. The assortment of vegetables looks quite appealing and healthy.

fried-food.flavor: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. It’s been a while since it was fried, so it’s gotten greasy.

boiled-egg.flavor: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. Even just one boiled egg can really enhance a dish.

camping-gear.flavor: Everything you need to set up a tent and get cooking in a Wild Area or at a camping spot.

rusted-sword.flavor: It is said that a hero used this sword to halt a terrible disaster in ancient times. But it’s grown rusty and worn.

rusted-shield.flavor: It is said that a hero used this shield to halt a terrible disaster in ancient times. But it’s grown rusty and worn.

fossilized-bird.flavor: The fossil of an ancient Pokémon that once soared through the sky. What it looked like is a mystery.

fossilized-fish.flavor: The fossil of an ancient Pokémon that once lived in the sea. What it looked like is a mystery.

fossilized-drake.flavor: The fossil of an ancient Pokémon that once roamed the land. What it looked like is a mystery.

fossilized-dino.flavor: The fossil of an ancient Pokémon that once lived in the sea. What it looked like is a mystery.

strawberry-sweet.flavor: A strawberry-shaped sweet. When a Milcery holds this, it will spin around happily.

love-sweet.flavor: A heart-shaped sweet. When a Milcery holds this, it spins around happily.

berry-sweet.flavor: A berry-shaped sweet. When a Milcery holds this, it spins around happily.

clover-sweet.flavor: A clover-shaped sweet. When a Milcery holds this, it spins around happily.

flower-sweet.flavor: A flower-shaped sweet. When a Milcery holds this, it spins around happily.

star-sweet.flavor: A star-shaped sweet. When a Milcery holds this, it spins around happily.

ribbon-sweet.flavor: A ribbon-shaped sweet. When a Milcery holds this, it spins around happily.

sweet-apple.flavor: A peculiar apple that can make a certain species of Pokémon evolve. It’s exceptionally sweet.

tart-apple.flavor: A peculiar apple that can make a certain species of Pokémon evolve. It’s exceptionally tart.

throat-spray.flavor: Raises Sp. Atk when a Pokémon uses a sound-based move.

eject-pack.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. When the holder’s stats are lowered, it will be switched out of battle.

heavy-duty-boots.flavor: These boots prevent the effects of traps set on the battlefield.

blunder-policy.flavor: Raises Speed sharply when a Pokémon misses with a move because of accuracy.

room-service.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. Lowers Speed when Trick Room takes effect.

utility-umbrella.flavor: An item to be held by a Pokémon. This sturdy umbrella protects the holder from the effects of rain and harsh sunlight.

exp-candy-xs.flavor: A candy that is packed with energy. When consumed, it will grant a single Pokémon a very small amount of Exp. Points.

exp-candy-s.flavor: A candy that is packed with energy. When consumed, it will grant a single Pokémon a small amount of Exp. Points.

exp-candy-m.flavor: A candy that is packed with energy. When consumed, it will grant a single Pokémon a moderate amount of Exp. Points.

exp-candy-l.flavor: A candy that is packed with energy. When consumed, it will grant a single Pokémon a large amount of Exp. Points.

exp-candy-xl.flavor: A candy that is packed with energy. When consumed, it will grant a single Pokémon a very large amount of Exp. Points.

dynamax-candy.flavor: A candy that is packed with energy. When consumed, it will raise the Dynamax Level of a single Pokémon by one. A higher level means higher HP when Dynamaxed.

tr00.flavor: A frenetic dance to uplift the fighting spirit. This sharply raises the user’s Attack stat.

tr01.flavor: The user drops onto the target with its full body weight. This may also leave the target with paralysis.

tr02.flavor: The target is scorched with an intense blast of fire. This may also leave the target with a burn.

tr03.flavor: The target is blasted by a huge volume of water launched under great pressure.

tr04.flavor: The user attacks everything around it by swamping its surroundings with a giant wave.

tr05.flavor: The target is struck with an icy-cold beam of energy. This may also leave the target frozen.

tr06.flavor: A howling blizzard is summoned to strike opposing Pokémon. This may also leave the opposing Pokémon frozen.

tr07.flavor: A powerful low kick that makes the target fall over. The heavier the target, the greater the move’s power.

tr08.flavor: A strong electric blast crashes down on the target. This may also leave the target with paralysis.

tr09.flavor: A wicked thunderbolt is dropped on the target to inflict damage. This may also leave the target with paralysis.

tr10.flavor: The user sets off an earthquake that strikes every Pokémon around it.

tr11.flavor: The target is hit by a strong telekinetic force. This may also lower the target’s Sp. Def stat.

tr12.flavor: The user relaxes and lightens its body to move faster. This sharply raises the Speed stat.

tr13.flavor: The user takes a deep breath and focuses so that critical hits land more easily.

tr14.flavor: The user waggles a finger and stimulates its brain into randomly using nearly any move.

tr15.flavor: The target is attacked with an intense blast of all-consuming fire. This may also leave the target with a burn.

tr16.flavor: The user charges at the target and may make it flinch.

tr17.flavor: The user temporarily empties its mind to forget its concerns. This sharply raises the user’s Sp. Def stat.

tr18.flavor: The user drains the target’s blood. The user’s HP is restored by half the damage taken by the target.

tr19.flavor: The user strikes with a simultaneous three-beam attack. This may also burn, freeze, or paralyze the target.

tr20.flavor: The user creates a substitute for itself using some of its HP. The substitute serves as the user’s decoy.

tr21.flavor: An all-out attack that becomes more powerful the less HP the user has.

tr22.flavor: Unsanitary sludge is hurled at the target. This may also poison the target.

tr23.flavor: The user lays a trap of spikes at the opposing team’s feet. The trap hurts Pokémon that switch into battle.

tr24.flavor: The user rampages and attacks for two to three turns. The user then becomes confused.

tr25.flavor: The user materializes an odd psychic wave to attack the target. This attack does physical damage.

tr26.flavor: The user endures any attack with at least 1 HP. Its chance of failing rises if it is used in succession.

tr27.flavor: While it is asleep, the user randomly uses one of the moves it knows.

tr28.flavor: Using its tough and impressive horn, the user rams into the target with no letup.

tr29.flavor: The user switches places with a party Pokémon in waiting and passes along any stat changes.

tr30.flavor: The user compels the target to keep using the move it encored for three turns.

tr31.flavor: The target is slammed with a steel-hard tail. This may also lower the target’s Defense stat.

tr32.flavor: The user crunches up the target with sharp fangs. This may also lower the target’s Defense stat.

tr33.flavor: The user hurls a shadowy blob at the target. This may also lower the target’s Sp. Def stat.

tr34.flavor: Two turns after this move is used, a hunk of psychic energy attacks the target.

tr35.flavor: The user attacks in an uproar for three turns. During that time, no Pokémon can fall asleep.

tr36.flavor: The user attacks by exhaling hot breath on opposing Pokémon. This may also leave those Pokémon with a burn.

tr37.flavor: The target is taunted into a rage that allows it to use only attack moves for three turns.

tr38.flavor: The user catches the target off guard and swaps its held item with its own.

tr39.flavor: The user attacks the target with great power. However, this also lowers the user’s Attack and Defense stats.

tr40.flavor: The user employs its psychic power to exchange Abilities with the target.

tr41.flavor: The user launches a kick that lands a critical hit more easily. This may also leave the target with a burn.

tr42.flavor: The user lets loose a horribly echoing shout with the power to inflict damage.

tr43.flavor: The user attacks the target at full power. The attack’s recoil harshly lowers the user’s Sp. Atk stat.

tr44.flavor: The user absorbs a mystical power from space to raise its Defense and Sp. Def stats.

tr45.flavor: The user attacks by shooting muddy water at opposing Pokémon. This may also lower their accuracy.

tr46.flavor: The user hardens its body’s surface like iron, sharply raising its Defense stat.

tr47.flavor: The user slashes the target with huge sharp claws.

tr48.flavor: The user tenses its muscles to bulk up its body, raising both its Attack and Defense stats.

tr49.flavor: The user quietly focuses its mind and calms its spirit to raise its Sp. Atk and Sp. Def stats.

tr50.flavor: The user handles a sharp leaf like a sword and attacks by cutting its target. Critical hits land more easily.

tr51.flavor: The user vigorously performs a mystic, powerful dance that raises its Attack and Speed stats.

tr52.flavor: The user tackles the target with a high-speed spin. The slower the user compared to the target, the greater the move’s power.

tr53.flavor: The user fights the target up close without guarding itself. This also lowers the user’s Defense and Sp. Def stats.

tr54.flavor: The user lays a trap of poison spikes at the feet of the opposing team. The spikes will poison opposing Pokémon that switch into battle.

tr55.flavor: The user cloaks itself in fire and charges the target. This also damages the user quite a lot. This attack may leave the target with a burn.

tr56.flavor: The user lets loose a blast of aura power from deep within its body at the target. This attack never misses.

tr57.flavor: The target is stabbed with a tentacle, arm, or the like steeped in poison. This may also poison the target.

tr58.flavor: The user releases a horrible aura imbued with dark thoughts. This may also make the target flinch.

tr59.flavor: The user slams a barrage of hard-shelled seeds down on the target from above.

tr60.flavor: The user slashes at the target by crossing its scythes or claws as if they were a pair of scissors.

tr61.flavor: The user generates a damaging sound wave by vibration. This may also lower the target’s Sp. Def stat.

tr62.flavor: The target is attacked with a shock wave generated by the user’s gaping mouth.

tr63.flavor: The user attacks with a ray of light that sparkles as if it were made of gemstones.

tr64.flavor: The user heightens its mental focus and unleashes its power. This may also lower the target’s Sp. Def stat.

tr65.flavor: The user draws power from nature and fires it at the target. This may also lower the target’s Sp. Def stat.

tr66.flavor: The user tucks in its wings and charges from a low altitude. This also damages the user quite a lot.

tr67.flavor: The user makes the ground under the target erupt with power. This may also lower the target’s Sp. Def stat.

tr68.flavor: The user stimulates its brain by thinking bad thoughts. This sharply raises the user’s Sp. Atk stat.

tr69.flavor: The user focuses its willpower to its head and attacks the target. This may also make the target flinch.

tr70.flavor: The user gathers all its light energy and releases it all at once. This may also lower the target’s Sp. Def stat.

tr71.flavor: The user whips up a storm of leaves around the target. The attack’s recoil harshly lowers the user’s Sp. Atk stat.

tr72.flavor: The user violently whirls its vines, tentacles, or the like to harshly lash the target.

tr73.flavor: The user shoots filthy garbage at the target to attack. This may also poison the target.

tr74.flavor: The user slams the target with its steel-hard head. This may also make the target flinch.

tr75.flavor: The user stabs the target from below with sharpened stones. Critical hits land more easily.

tr76.flavor: The user lays a trap of levitating stones around the opposing team. The trap hurts opposing Pokémon that switch into battle.

tr77.flavor: The user snares the target with grass and trips it. The heavier the target, the greater the move’s power.

tr78.flavor: The user strikes everything around it by swamping the area with a giant sludge wave. This may also poison those hit.

tr79.flavor: The user slams into the target with its heavy body. The more the user outweighs the target, the greater the move’s power.

tr80.flavor: The user hurls an electric orb at the target. The faster the user is than the target, the greater the move’s power.

tr81.flavor: The user turns the target’s power against it. The higher the target’s Attack stat, the greater the damage it deals.

tr82.flavor: The user attacks the target with stored power. The more the user’s stats are raised, the greater the move’s power.

tr83.flavor: The user teleports using a strange power and switches places with one of its allies.

tr84.flavor: The user shoots boiling hot water at its target. This may also leave the target with a burn.

tr85.flavor: The user is roused, and its Attack and Sp. Atk stats increase.

tr86.flavor: The user shrouds itself in electricity and smashes into its target. This also damages the user a little.

tr87.flavor: The user crashes into its target while rotating its body like a drill. Critical hits land more easily.

tr88.flavor: The user slams its target with its flame-covered body. The more the user outweighs the target, the greater the move’s power.

tr89.flavor: The user attacks by wrapping its opponent in a fierce wind that flies up into the sky. This may also confuse the target.

tr90.flavor: The user plays rough with the target and attacks it. This may also lower the target’s Attack stat.

tr91.flavor: Opposing Pokémon are drenched in an odd poisonous liquid. This lowers the Attack, Sp. Atk, and Speed stats of a poisoned target.

tr92.flavor: The user damages opposing Pokémon by emitting a powerful flash.

tr93.flavor: The user swings both arms and hits the target. The target’s stat changes don’t affect this attack’s damage.

tr94.flavor: The user fiercely attacks the target using its entire body.

tr95.flavor: The user attacks the target’s throat, and the resultant suffering prevents the target from using moves that emit sound for two turns.

tr96.flavor: The user attacks the enemy with a pollen puff that explodes. If the target is an ally, it gives the ally a pollen puff that restores its HP instead.

tr97.flavor: The user bites the target with its psychic capabilities. This can also destroy Light Screen and Reflect.

tr98.flavor: The user slams into the target using a full-force blast of water. This may also lower the target’s Defense stat.

tr99.flavor: The user attacks by slamming its body into the target. The higher the user’s Defense, the more damage it can inflict on the target.

tm00.flavor: The target is slugged by a punch thrown with muscle-packed power.

serious-mint.flavor: When a Pokémon smells this mint, all of its stats will grow at an equal rate.

wishing-piece.flavor: Throw one into a Pokémon Den to attract a wild Dynamax Pokémon.

cracked-pot.flavor: A peculiar teapot that can make a certain species of Pokémon evolve. It may be cracked, but tea poured from it is delicious.

chipped-pot.flavor: A peculiar teapot that can make a certain species of Pokémon evolve. It may be chipped, but tea poured from it is delicious.

hi-tech-earbuds.flavor: Strange earbuds that allow you to freely control the volume of various sounds.

fruit-bunch.flavor: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. The syrupy fruits give off a strong tropical feel.

moomoo-cheese.flavor: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. Melted cheese can really give a curry some richness.

spice-mix.flavor: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. There are over fifty spices contained within this ingredient’s hot flavor.

fresh-cream.flavor: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. Add some whipped cream to a spicy curry to give it some sweetness.

packaged-curry.flavor: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. Using packaged curry helps make sure there are no slipups when cooking.

coconut-milk.flavor: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. The refined sweetness of this coconut milk makes it quite popular.

instant-noodles.flavor: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. It has a junk-food sort of taste, but it goes well with curry.

precooked-burger.flavor: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. If you’re unsure about what to cook, you can’t go wrong with some burger.

gigantamix.flavor: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. A mysterious spice. Even a tiny sprinkle is sure to make a curry gigantic.

catching-charm.flavor: Holding it is said to increase the chance of getting a critical catch. Curiously, the charm doesn’t shake much.

rotom-catalog.flavor: A catalog of devices that Rotom like. Use the catalog to have Rotom hop in and out of the various devices listed within.

max-honey.flavor: Honey that Dynamax Vespiquen produces. Adding this honey to Max Soup makes the taste very smooth. It also has the same effect as a Max Revive.

max-mushrooms.flavor: Mushrooms that have the power of changing Dynamax forms. They boost all stats of a Pokémon during battle.

galarica-twig.flavor: A twig from a species of tree called Galarica that grows in certain areas around the Galar region. It’s a material for an accessory for a certain Pokémon.

galarica-cuff.flavor: A cuff made from woven-together Galarica Twigs. Giving it to Galarian Slowpoke makes the Pokémon very happy.

style-card.flavor: A card that increases the selection in boutiques and hair salons.

exp-charm.flavor: A charm that increases the Exp. Points that Pokémon can get. A machine-like object is inside the charm.

armorite-ore.flavor: Rare ore that can be obtained on the Isle of Armor. Something good will happen if you give it to a collector on the Isle of Armor.

mark-charm.flavor: A mysterious and unique charm that increases the chance of finding a Pokémon with a mark.

galarica-wreath.flavor: A wreath made from woven-together Galarica Twigs. Placing it on the head of a Galarian Slowpoke makes the Pokémon very happy.

dynite-ore.flavor: Mysterious ore that can be obtained in the Max Lair. Something good will happen if you give it to a collector in the Crown Tundra.

ability-patch.flavor: A patch that allows a Pokémon with a regular Ability to have a rare Ability.
